pages.text: 276-15  December 15, 2015  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the Township of West Orange is desirous of awarding a contract for  providing recreational, social and educational services to the Senior Citizens of the  Township   of West Orange, for the period commencing January 1, 2015 and ending  December 31, 2015  WHEREAS ,   the following organizations are willing to provide said services;  Pleasantdale Senior Citizens  JCC Council of Senior Citizens  The   J   o   h   n   Renna House  Eagle Rock Senior Citizens H.A./Tenant Services  Woodland Valley Senior Social Gathering  Knitting Club  Prime Timers  WHEREAS,   the aggregate of public funds to be expended for the provision of  such services is $2,000.00 and therefore, no public advertisements for bids is required.  N OW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED   by the Township Council of the  Township of West Orange that the Mayor and Township Clerk be and hereby  respectively authorized to execute and attest to a contract, in a form approved by the  Township Attorney, with the Pleasantdale Senior Citizens, JCC   C   o   u   n   c   i   l   Senior Citizens,  The John P. Renna House, Eagle Rock Senior Citizens, Woodland Valley Senior Social  Gathering,   Prime Timers and The Knitting Club for the provisions of recreational,  educational, informational and social services to the Senior Citizens of the Township of  West Orange.  Karen   J. Carnevale   Jerry Guarino  Township Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   December 15, 2015  I hereby certify funds available   01-2010-00-8762-080   ________________________________  John O. Gross, Chief Financial Officer  4850-3195-7547, v. 1

documents.title: - TAX EXEMPTION DISABLED VET 376 ST CLOUD

pages.text: 277-15  December 15, 2015  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   STEVEN   SCULLAWL,   is   the   owner   of   the   single   family   dwelling   at   376  St. Cloud Avenue (Block 159.11, Lot 16); and  WHEREAS,   STEVEN   SCULLAWL,   is   a   totally   disabled   veteran   entitled   to   exemption  from real estate taxes pursuant to   N.J.S.   54:4-3.30 et seq; and  WHEREAS,   STEVEN   SCULLAWL,   has   been   granted   total   exemption   from   real   estate  taxes on July 29, 2015;  NOW   THEREFORE   BE   IT   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE   that   STEVEN   SCULLAWL   be   granted   exemption   from   real  estate taxes, as a totally disabled veteran, pursuant to   N.J.S.   54:4-3.30 et seq.  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED   that   the   Tax   Assessor   and   Tax   Collector   adjust   their  records to reflect the aforementioned grant of exemption.  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED   that   the   Tax   Collector   refund   the   sum   of   $4,376.88   to  STEVE   SCULLAWL   which   sum   represents   real   estate   taxes   paid   for   July   29,   2015   to   November  2015.  Karen J. Carnevale   Jerry Guarino  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   December 15, 2015

documents.title: RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING RENEWAL OF DELTA DENTAL PLAN FOR 2016

pages.text: 283-15  December 15, 2015  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   on   or   about   September   17,   2013,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   Council  authorized   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   to   enter   into   a   Contract   for   Employee  Dental Insurance (the   “ Agreement ” ) with Delta Dental of New Jersey, Inc. ( “ Delta ” ) ; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   seeks   to   renew   the   Agreement   with   Delta   for   a   one-year   term  (the   “ Term ” )   from January 1, 2016 through December 31, 2016 (the   “ Amendment ” ) ; and  WHEREAS,   the   monthly   guaranteed   rates   for   the   Term   are   set   forth   in   the   Amendment ,  annexed hereto as Attachment   “ A; ”  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   that   the   Township   shall   and   hereby   does   authorize   the  Amendment , at the guaranteed rates set forth   therein ; and be it further  RESOLVED,   that   the   Mayor   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   execute   all   documents  necessary to effectuate   the   Amendment ; and be it further  RESOLVED   that   this   Resolution   shall   be   published   and   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s  Office for reasonable inspection in accordance with applicable law.  Karen J. C arnevale   Jerry Guarino  Township Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   December 15, 2015  I hereby certify funds are available from :  Account No.  John Gross, Chief Financial Officer

284-15  December 15, 2015  R E S O L U T I O N  ESSEX COUNTY COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT BLOCK GRANT  (CDBG)   PROGRAM   YEAR   201 6  WHEREAS , certain Federal funding will potentially be available for Essex  County under Title I of the Housing and Community Development Act of 1974 as  amended and supplemented, which Act is commonly known and referred to as the  Community Development Block Grant; and  WHEREAS , the Township of West Orange has a need and desire for assistance  under the Act and Program as aforesaid in order to improve essential services to the  residents of areas which qualify for assistance under the Act and Program as  aforementioned.  NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED   the Township Council of the  Township of West Orange, authorizes, on behalf of the Township of West Orange, the  submission of applications for funding of the attached list, known as the   20 1 6  Community Development Block Grant Program list, in the priority order as shown.  Karen J. Carnevale   Jerry Guarino  Township Clerk   Council President  Adopted: December 15, 2015

286-15  December 15, 2015  RESOLUTION  JANITORIAL SERVICES FOR THE   TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  This Agreement for Janitorial Services is made by and between the Township of West Orange  located at Municipal Building, 66 Main Street, West Orange, New Jersey (herein the  “ Township ” ) and   Excellent Building Services, Corp. , located at   70 South Jefferson Street,  Orange, NJ 07050 .  WHEREAS, the Township requires janitorial services at the following buildings: (1) West  Orange Town Hall; (2) West Orange Police Building; (3) West Orange Senior Health Center; (4)  Department of Public Works/Engineering; (5) Department of Recreation; (6) Police Valley Road  Station; and (7) Police Washington Street Sub Station (collectively, the   “ Buildings ” ); and  WHEREAS, the Township issued a Request for Bid ( “ RFB ” ) in accordance with the Local  Public Contracts Law (N.J.S.A. 40A:11-1, et al.) for Janitorial Services for the Buildings;  WHEREAS, on   December 2, 2015   the Township received   seven (7)   bids in response to the RFB;  and  WHEREAS,   Excellent Building Services Corp.   is recommended as the   lowest   responsible and  responsive bidder.  NOW THEREFORE, for valuable consideration, including the promises, covenants,  representations and warranties hereinafter set forth, the receipt and adequacy of which are hereby  acknowledged, the parties, intending to be legally and equitably bound, agree as follows:  1. Term   –   The term of this Contract shall commence on January 1, 201 6   for a two (2) year period  with an option to renew for one (1) additional year. The Township will notify   Excellent Building  Services Corp.   at least 30 days prior to the expiration of the initial two year period if it desires to  exercise the option to renew for the additional one (1) year period.  2. Price   –   The amount of the Contract shall not exceed $ 4 4,376.00   annually.  Optional additional   floor   services @ hourly rate of $25.00  Optional interior painting @ hourly rate of $35.00  3. Other Provisions   – This Contract between   Excellent Building Services Corp.   and the Township  is subject to all terms and conditions contained in the Request for Bid and   Excellent Building  Services Corporation ’ s   bid response   incorporated   herein in full as though set forth in verbatim  and as if they were fully reiterated in the body of this Contract.  4. Notice   –   Any notices required hereunder to the Township shall be hand delivered or mailed by  certified first class postage prepaid return receipt required mail to the Municipal Clerk, Township  of West Orange, New Jersey 07052 or such other address as the Township may designate in  writing to   Excellent Building Services Corporation.   Any notice required hereunder to the

Excellent Building Services   Corporation   shall be hand delivered or mailed by   certified first -class  postage prepaid return receipt required mail to   Excellent Building Services   Corp oration , 70 South  Jefferson Street, Orange, NJ 07050   or such other address as   Excellent Building Services  Corporation   may designate in writing to the Township.  5. Captions   –   The captions appearing in the Agreement or of any copies thereof are inserted only  as matter of convenience and for reference, and in no way define, limit or describe the scope or  intent of this Agreement nor in any way affect this Agreement.  6. Governing Law   –   This Agreement shall be construed in accordance with the laws of the State  of New Jersey without regard to conflicts of law principles. The parties consent to the personal  and subject matter jurisdiction of the Superior Court of Jersey for resolution of any and all claims  arising out of or in connection with this C ontract.  IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties hereto have hereunto set their hands and seals the day and  year first above written.  Jerry Guarino , Council President  ATTEST:   TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  By:  Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk   Robert D. Parisi, Mayor  WITNESS:   EXCELLENT BUILDING  SERVICES, CORP.  By:  Title:  I hereby certify funds to be available:  Account #01-2010-00-7101-071  Subject to 2016 Budget Approval  John O. Gross, Chief Financial Officer

documents.title: RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING RENEWAL OF JIF MEMBERSHIP 2016

pages.text: 289-15  December 15, 2015  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   a   number   of   local   governmental   units   in   the   State   of   New   Jersey   have  joined   together   to   form   a   Joint   Insurance   Fund   as   permitted   by   the   Chapter   372   Laws   of   1983  (40A:10-36); and  WHEREAS,   the   statutes   and   regulations   governing   the   creation   and   operation   of   a   Joint  Insurance   Fund   contain   elaborate   restrictions   and   safeguards   concerning   the   safe   and   efficient  administration of the public interest entrusted to such a Fund; and  WHEREAS,   the   Governing   Body   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange   has   determined   that  membership   in   the   Garden   State   Municipal   Joint   Insurance   Fund   (hereinafter   referred   to   as   the  “ Fund ” ) is in the best interests of itself and its citizens; and  WHEREAS,   the   Governing   Body   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange   has   previously  executed   a   resolution   to   join   the   Garden   State   Municipal   Joint   Insurance   Fund   subject   to   the  acceptance of its assessment;  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   that   the   Governing   Body   of   the   Township   of  West   Orange   (hereinafter   the   “ Municipality ” )   does   hereby   resolve   and   agree   to   renew   its  membership   in   the   Fund   for   a   period   not   to   exceed   one   (1)   year   beginning   January   1,   2016,   and  ending December 31, 2016,   for the purpose of establishing the following types of coverage:     Workers ’   Compensation and Employer ’ s Liability;     General Liability including law enforcement and public officials liability;     Motor vehicle liability;   and     Property damage (buildings, contents, equipment, and motor vehicle);  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED,   that   the   Municipality   has   never   defaulted   on   claims   and  has   not   been   canceled   for   nonpayment   of   insurance   premiums   for   a   period   of   at   least   two   (2)  years prior to the adoption of this Resolution; and

2  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED,   that   the   Mayor   and   the   Municipal   Clerk   are   authorized  and   directed   to   execute   the   Indemnity   and   Trust   Agreement   in   the   form   annexed   hereto   as  Attachment   “ A ”   and   such   other   documents   signifying   membership   in   the   Fund   and   to   deliver  same   to   the   Executive   Director   of   the   Fund   with   the   express   reservation   that   said   document  shall   become   effective   only   upon   approval   by   the   New   Jersey   Department   of   Insurance   and   the  Department of Community Affairs.  Karen J.   Carnevale   Jerry Guarino  Township Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   December 15, 2015  Approved as to form on the basis of the facts provided:  Richard D. Trenk  Township Attorney  I hereby certify funds are available from:  Account No.  John Gross, Chief Financial Officer  ADOPTED:  I hereby certify that the above is a true and exact copy of the Resolution adopted by the  Municipal Council of the   Township of West Orange   at their Regular Meeting held on  December 15, 2015 .  Signature/Date  4821-1814-3020, v. 1

#2161968 v1  100181-33129  290-15  December 15, 2015  RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING EMERGENCY APPROPRIATION PURSUANT TO  N.J.S.A. 40A:4-46   AND N.J.S.A. 40A:4-4 8   DUE TO   HEALTH BENEFIT CLAIMS  WHEREAS, an emergency has arisen with respects to   expenses related to   meet expenses  related to substantial unanticipated and unforeseen   health benefit claims   and no adequate  provision was made in the   201 5   budget for the aforesaid purpose, and N.J.S. 40A:4-46 provides  for the creation of an emergency appropriation for the purpose above mentioned, and  WHEREAS, the total amount of emergency appropriation s   created including the  appropriation to be created by this resolution is $ 1,500,00.00   and three per cent of the total  operations in the budget for the year   201 5   is $ 1, 903 , 595.01 , and  WHEREAS, the foregoing appropriation together with prior appropriations   does   not  exceed three percent of the total current appropriations in the budget for the year   201 5 ,  NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED   BY THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL OF THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST ORANGE , IN THE COUNTY OF   ESSEX , NEW JERSEY   (not less  than two-thirds of all the members thereof affirmatively concurring) , AS FOLLOWS:  1 .   An emergency appropriation be and the same is hereby made   pursuant to N.J.S.A. 40A :4 -  46   for funding   of   expenses related to   meet expenses related to substantial unanticipated  and unforeseen   health benefit claims   in the amount of $ 1,500,000.00 .  2 .   That said emergency appropriation shall be provided in full in the 20 1 6   budget, except if  financing of such emergency appropriation shall have been made   from other funds or   by  authorization of   notes or   bonds pursuant to N.J.S.A.   40A:2-3 or   40A:2- 51 .  3 .   That two certified copies of this resolution be filed with the Director of Local  Government Services.  Karen J. Carnevale   Jerry Guarino  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted: December 15, 2015

#2161969 v1  100181-33129  291-15  December 15, 2015  RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING   SALE OF NOTES FOR  EMERGENCY APPROPRIATION PURSUANT TO N.J.S.A. 40A:4- 51  DUE TO   HEALTH BENEFIT CLAIMS  WHEREAS, a   resolution   has been adopted creating an   emergency   appropriation of  $ 1,500,000.00   to meet expenses related   to   substantial   unanticipated and unforeseen   health  benefit claims ,   and N.J.S. 40A:4-46 provides for the creation of an emergency appropriation for  the purpose above mentioned, and  NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED   BY THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL OF  THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WE S T ORANGE , IN THE COUNTY OF   ESSEX , NEW JERSEY   (not  less than two-thirds of all the members thereof affirmatively concurring) , AS FOLLOWS:  1.   An   emergency   exists   and   the   emergency   appropriation   provided   for   herein   is  necessary   in   order   to   meet   expenses   related   to   substantial   unanticipated   and  unforeseen   health benefit claims.  2.   In   order   to   finance   the   emergency   appropriation   described   in   this   resolution,   the  Township   is   hereby   authorized   to   borrow   the   sum   of   $  1,500,000.00   and   to   issue   its  emergency   notes   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40A:4-51.   The   emergency   notes   authorized   by   this  resolution   may   be   issued   in   amounts   and   denominations   not   exceeding   in   the   aggregate  the   amount   of   notes   authorized   herein,   as   determined   by   the   chief   financial   officer   of   the  Township   pursuant to this resolution.  3.   The following matters in connection with the notes are hereby determined:  (a)   All   notes   issued   hereunder   and   any   renewals   thereof   shall   mature   at   such  time   as   may   be   determined   by   the   chief   financial   officer ,   provided,  however,   that   the   notes   shall   have   a   final   maturity   and   be   paid   not   later  than   the   last   day   of   the   next   succeeding   fiscal   year   following   the   date   of  this resolution ;  (b)   All   notes   issued   hereunder   shall   bear   interest   at   such   rate   or   rates   as   may  be determined by the chief financial officer;  (c)   The   notes   shall   be   in   the   form   determined   by   the   chief   financial   officer   and  the   chief   financial   officer's   signature   upon   the   notes   shall   be   conclusive   as  to such determination.  4.   The   chief   financial   officer   is   hereby   authorized   and   directed   to   determine   all   matters  in   connection   with   the   notes   not   determined   by   this   or   a   subsequent   resolution   and   the   chief  financial officer's signature upon the notes shall be conclusive as to such determination.

#2161969 v1  100181-33129  5.   The   chief   financial   officer   is   hereby   authorized   to   sell   the   notes   and   any   renewals  thereof   from   time   to   time   at   public   or   private   sale   in   such   amounts   as   such   officer   may  determine   at   not   less   than   par   and   to   deliver   the   same   from   time   to   time   to   the   purchasers  thereof   upon   receipt   of   payment   of   the   purchase   price   plus   accrued   interest   from   their   dates  to the date of delivery thereof and payment therefor.  6.   All   notes   issued   pursuant   to   this   resolution   shall   be   executed   by   the   chief   financial   officer  and   the   Mayor   and   shall   be   under   the   seal   of   the   Township   and   attested   by   the   Clerk   of  the   Township .   Such   officers   are   hereby   authorized   to   execute   and   deliver   all   documents  necessary or convenient in connection with the issuance, sale and delivery of the notes.  7.   Any   note   issued   pursuant   to   this   resolution   shall   be   a   general   obligation   of   the  Township  .   The   full   faith   and   credit   of   the   Township   are   hereby   pledged   to   the   punctual  payment   of   the   principal   of   and   the   interest   on   the   notes   authorized   herein   and,   unless  otherwise   paid   or   payment   is   provided   for,   an   amount   sufficient   for   such   payment   shall   be  inserted   in   the   budget   and   a   tax   sufficient   to   provide   for   the   payment   thereof   shall   be  levied and collected.  8.   The   chief   financial   officer   is   authorized   and   directed   to   report   in   writing   to   this  governing   body   at   the   meeting   next   succeeding   the   date   when   any   sale   or   delivery   of   the  notes   pursuant   to   this   resolution   is   made,   such   report   to   include   the   amount,   description,  interest   rate   and   maturity   of   the   notes   sold,   the   price   obtained   and   the   name   of   the  purchaser.  Karen J. Carnevale   Jerry Guarino  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted: December 15, 2015

pages.text: 292-15  December 15, 2015  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   promulgates   and   adheres   to  Human   Resources   Policies   and   Procedures   ( “ HRPP ” )   in   connection   with   general   administrative  matters; and  WHEREAS,   in its current form, Section 5:2-1 of the HRPP states:   “ It   is   the   policy   of   the  Township   of   West   Orange   to   pay   employees   weekly   in   accordance   with   established   payroll  periods ” ; and  WHEREAS,   in   its   current   form,   Section   5:2-2 ( a )   states:   “ There   are   52   paydays   in   a   year.  The   payday   is   normally   based   on   the   last   day   of   the   prevailing   year.   For   example,   if   December  31 st   falls on a Thursday, the payday throughout that calendar year will be Thursday ” ; and  WHEREAS,   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40A:5-19,   the   Township   is   permitted   to   provide   for   the  bi-weekly   payment   of   salaries,   wages   and   compensation   of   officers   and   employees,   both  elective and appointive; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   desires   to   transition   from   weekly   payment   of   salaries   to   bi-  weekly   payment   of   salaries   for   all   employees ,   effective   for   all   payroll   periods   subsequent   to  December 25, 2015; and  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   that   the   Township   shall   pay   all   employees   on   a   bi-weekly  basis, effective for all payroll periods subsequent to December 25, 2015 ; and be it further  RESOLVED,   that   Section   5:2-1   of   the   HRPP   shall   be   amended   to   state:   “ It   is   the   policy  of   the   Township   of   West   Orange   to   pay   employees   bi-weekly   in   accordance   with   established  payroll periods ” ; and it is further

2  RESOLVED,   that   Section   5:2-2(a)   of   the   HRPP   shall   be   deleted   in   its   entirety;   and   it   is  further  RESOLVED ,   that   this   Resolution   shall   be   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   Office   for  reasonable inspection in accordance with applicable law.  Karen   J.   Carnevale   Jerry Guarino  Township Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   December 15, 2015  4826-7022-1100, v. 1

2471-15  AN ORDINANCE AMENDING   CHAPTER 2 , SECTION   52.2 OF THE REVISED  GENERAL ORDINANCES OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE, ENTITLED  “ COMPOSITION OF COMMISSION ”  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   ORDAINED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE ,   in   the   County   of   Essex   and   State   of   New   Jersey,   that  Chapter   2,   Section   52.2   of   the   Revised   General   Ordinances   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange  shall be   amended   and superseded   as follows:  2- 52 PUBLIC   RELATIONS COMMISSION  2- 52.2 Composition   of Commission.  The   Commission   shall   be   composed   of   eight   (8)   members   each   of   whom   shall   be   bona  fide   residents   of   West   Orange.   Each   member   of   the   Township   Council   shall   appoint   one   (1)  member   to   the   Commission   and   the   Mayor   shall   appoint   three   (3)   members   to   the   Commission  each   to   serve   a   one   (1)   year   term   renewable   at   expiration   for   the   remainder   of   the   elected  officials   term.   The   members   shall   be   commissioned   by   the   submission   of   a   letter   to   the  Township   Clerk's   office.   All   vacancies   shall   be   filled   for   the   unexpired   term   only.   Each   member  of   the   Township   Council   and   the   Mayor   shall   reappoint   their   Commissioner   upon   reelection   or  appoint   new   Commissioners.   Each   Public   Relations   Commissioner's   term   expires   with   the  elected   official   who   nominated   them.   The   Township   Council   shall   appoint   one   (1)   Council  member to serve as a liaison to the Commission each year.  Commissioners   may   be   recommended   for   removal   from   the   Commission   for   acts   or  deeds   considered   by   the   Public   Relations   Commission,   Township   Council   or   Mayor   as   contrary  to   the   aims   and   goals   of   the   Public   Relations   Commission;   three   (3)   consecutive   missed  regularly   scheduled   Public   Relations   Commission   meetings   or   attendance   at   less   than   two-  thirds (2/3) of the yearly meetings.  To   remove   a   Public   Relations   Commissioner   from   office   a   written   explanation   of   the  reason   for   said   removal   shall   be   submitted   to   the   Council   President,   Mayor,   nominating   Elected  Official   (if   not   one   of   the   previous)   and   Public   Relations   Commissioner   involved   by   the  Chairperson   of   the   Public   Relations   Commission.   Upon   receipt   of   the   notice   from   the  Commission   the   nominating   elected   official   may   remove   and   replace   said   Commissioner.   A  replacement   shall   be   selected   by   said   nominator   to   finish   the   vacated,   unexpired   term.   (Ord.  1893-03 § 2; Ord. No. 2170-08 § II)  BE IT FURTHER ORDAINED , that the remainder of Chapter 2 shall remain unchanged.  BE   IT   FURTHER   ORDAINED,   that   if   any   section,   paragraph,   subsection,   clause   or  provision   of   this   Ordinance   shall   be   adjudged   by   the   courts   to   be   invalid,   such   adjudication   shall  apply   only   to   the   section,   paragraph,   subsection,   clause   or   provision   so   adjudicated,   and   the  remainder of the Ordinance shall be deemed valid and effective.

BE   IT   FURTHER   ORDAINED ,   that   any   ordinances   or   parts   thereof   in   conflict   with   the  provisions of this Ordinance are repealed to the extent of such conflict.  BE   IT   FURTHER   ORDAINED ,   that   this   Ordinance   shall   take   effect   upon   passage   and  publication in accordance with law.  Karen J. Carnevale   Jerry Guarino  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Robert D. Parisi, Mayor  Introduction: November 24, 2015  Adopted: December 15, 2015

2471-15  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This   Ordinance   is   intended   to   foster   a   close   working   relationship   between   the   Township  Council   and   the   Public   Relations   Commission .   To   ensure   this   working   relationship   continues,  the   amendment   to   Chapter   2,   Section   52.2   includes   the   Township   Council ’ s   appointment   of   one  Council member to serve as a liaison to the Commission each year.  4820-7567-3131, v. 1

pages.text: 271 -15  November 24 , 2015  RESOLUTION TO HOLD A CLOSED SESSION  WHEREAS , the Open Public Meetings Act (N.J.S.A. 10:4. et seq.) provides for the exclusion of  the public meetings of the governing body during the discussion of certain matters; and  WHEREAS , prior to the exclusion of the public from a meeting of the Township Council it is  required that the Council adopt a Resolution stating the general nature of the subject to be discussed and  stating as precisely as possible the time when the minutes of the discussion conducted in closed session   can  be disclosed to the public; and  WHERE AS , this body is about to consider a matter which falls within the purview of N.J.S.A.  10-4-12, and can properly exclude the public from such discussions; now, therefore, be it  BE IT   RESOLVED , that the Township Council of the Township of West Orange, now assembled  in public session   at   its regular meeting of   November 24, 2015   enter into a closed session to consider the  following matter(s).  ( )   1.   Discussion of any material, the disclosure of which constitutes an  invasion of individual privacy .  (   )   2 .   A collective bargaining agreement or the terms of provisions of same.  ( )   3.   Techniques or tactic utilized to protect the safety and property of the public provided that  their disclosure could impair such protection.  ( )   4.   An investigation   of violations o r   possible violations of the laws of the State of New  Jersey   and/ or   the Township of West Orange Administrative Code.  ( X )   5.   Pending or anticipated litigation or contract negotiation in which this body is   or maybe a  party.  ( )   6.   A matter falling within the attorney-client privilege to the extent that confidentiality is  r equired in order for the attorney to   exercise his ethical duties to this body.  ()   7.   Personnel matters.  (   )   8.   Discussion of any matter which by express provision of Federal law or State statute or  rule of Court shall be rendered confidential.  ( )   9.   Discussion   of any matter in which the release of information would impair   a right to  receive funds from the Government of the United Sates.  ( )   10 .   Discussion of any matter involving the purchase, lease or acquisition   of   real property  with public funds, the setting   of banking   rates or investment of public funds, where it  could adversely affect the public interest if discussion of such matters were disclosed.  ( )   11.   Deliberations occurring after a public hearing that may result in imposition of a civil  penalty or suspension or loss of a license or permit.

More specifically, the Council   may   discuss in closed session the following topic ( s ) :  Matter(s) of   Litigation  a.   271 -15 Resolution Authorizing an Executive Session for the Purpose   D iscussing Legal  Issues Relating To West Essex Highlands Property."  (the general subject matter ( s ) ) of discussion shall be indicated by a check mark in the appropriate box and  shall be more specifically referred to thereafter to the extent suc h   additional disclosure can be made without  compromising the need for confidentiality giving rise to the closed session.  IT IS FURTH E R   RESOLVED   that   the minutes of said discussion shall be made public as  soon as the matter under discussion is no longer of a confidential or sensitive nature such that the public  interest will no longer be served by such confidentiality.  The foregoing Resolution was duly adopted by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange at a  public meeting held on   November 24, 2015  Motion :  Second:  Vote  Aye:  Opposed:  Karen J. Carnevale   Jerry Guarino  M unicipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   November 24, 2015

272-15  November 24, 2015  RESOLUTION  AUTHORIZING   A   COMPETITIVE CONTRACTING PROCESS FOR  AN   ENERGY AGGREGATION CONSULTANT  W HEREAS,   the Township   of   West Orange is seeking a Company specializing in  Energy Aggregation Consulting Service s for   the purpose of providing the residents of  West Orange   w ith a Third Party   Supplier of Electricity, who can   provide a rate   lower  than   the   current   Utility Company   Public Service Electric and Gas (PSE&G)   and  WHEREAS ,   the Township ’ s current contract with Consolidated Edison Solutions for   the  third   party supply of electricity for our residents will expire   December 2015 ; and  WHEREAS,   an   Energy Aggregation Consultant   would   provide the Township with  services in connection with the development and administration of the energy procurement  process for a Government Energy Aggregation (GEA) program . Suppliers will be selected  through   an   on - line bidding   process   pursuant to   P . L .   2001, c. 30: N. J. S.A. 48:3-50-51;  and 48:3-92 et.   al .;   and  WHEREAS ,   the   township meets the criteria of   the   Local Public Contracts Law N.J.S.A.  40a:11-4.1 , etc.   which   allows   this   process   by which   the Township   will   advertise   for   a request  f or   p roposals .   The methodology for the awarding of competitive contracts shall be based on an  evaluation   and ranking, which shall include technical, management, and cost related criteria,  all developed   in a way that is intended to meet the specific needs of the Township.  NOW   HEREBY BE IT RESOLVED , by the Council of the Township of West Orange ,  th at   Competitive Contract Process shall be i nitiated   for the   above n amed   purpose and  implemented   by the Purchasing Agent   and   Administration   according to the   Local Public  Contracts Law.  Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk   Jerry Guarino, Council President  Adopted: November 24, 2015

273-15  November 24, 2015  R E S O L U T I O N  WHEREAS,   the Tow nship of West Orange proposes the   improve ment   of several streets as part  of the 201 6   Street Improvement Project   t o   be   funded in the 201 6   Capital Budget; and  WHEREAS,   to design and specify these improvements the Township requires the services of a  professional engineering   firm ; and  WHEREAS,   the Township Engineer prepared a Request for Proposal dated   November 5 , 201 5   for  these professional design services; and  WHEREAS,   the Township Engineer solicited proposals from   three   professional engineering and  land surveying firms:   Crew Engineers, Inc. of Butler, N.J., Ke ller and Kirkpatrick, Inc., of Morris Plains,  N.J.;   and   Suburban Consulting Engineers, Inc. of Mt. Arlington, N.J. ;   and  WHEREAS,   all   three   firms responded to the Request for Proposals; and  WHEREAS,   the Township Engineer upon review of the proposals determined all   three   firms were  responsive; and  WHEREAS,   all   three   firms previously provided professional engineering services to the Township  and their work quality was excellent; and  WHEREAS,   by letter dated   November 18,   201 5   the Township Engineer recommended a  professional services contract be awarded to   Crew Engineers, Inc. ,   of Butler,   N.J. for the design ,  specifications and bid documents of   201 6   Street Improvement in accordance with their proposal dated  November 13 , 201 5   in an amount not to exceed $ 26, 4 0 0.00 .   and  WHEREAS,   sufficient funds exist for the services outlined in the proposal of   Crew Engineers, Inc. ;  and  WHEREAS,   the Township Council concurs in the foregoing.

NOW THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED   by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange,  New Jersey that a professional services contract be awarded to   Crew Engineers, Inc. ,   of 1250 Route 23  North, Butler, New Jersey   07 405   to provide professional engineering services for the 201 6   Street  Improvements in accordance with their proposal dated   November 13,   201 5   for a fee not to exceed  $ 26, 4 00.00.  BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED   that a copy of this Resolution be published in the West Orange  Chronicle within ten (10) days of its approval as required by State Statute. 274-15  November 24, 2015  R E S O L U T I O N  WHEREAS , the Township Council by Resolution No. 215-15 dated August 11, 2015 awarded a  Contract   to Reggio Construction Inc., 1575 West Street, Fort Lee, New Jersey 07024 for Street  Improvements , Phase II   –   2015 in the amount of $3,086,160.53; and  WHEREAS,   one of the streets to be improved is Barry Drive; and  WHEREAS,   the residents of Barry Drive requested   of   the Township the installation of traffic  calming speed tables since two parallel roads, Conforti Avenue and Fitzrandolph Road, have speed  tables; and  WHEREAS,   the Municipal Engineer prepared a traffic calming plan for Barry Drive that proposed  three speed tables on Barry Drive; and  WHEREAS,   resident s   of Barry Drive overwhelmingly approved the plan; and  WHEREAS,   Reggio Construction, Inc. quoted a cost of $5,000 per speed table which includes  materials, labor, signs and traffic makings; and  WHEREAS,   this cost is acceptable to the Municipal Engineer; and  WHEREAS,   the Municipal Engineer prepared Change Order No. 1 with Reggio Construction, Inc.  dated November 18, 2015 in the amount of $15,000   for this additional work ; and  WHEREAS,   sufficient funds exist for this additional work; and  WHEREAS,   the Township Council concurs with the foregoing.

NOW THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED   by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange  that Change Order No. 1 with Reggio Construction, Inc., 1575 West Street, Fort Lee, New Jersey 07024  for the project Street Improvements Phase II   –   2015 dated November 18, 2015 in the amount of  $15,000.00 which raises the contract amount from $3,086,160.53 to $3,101,160.53 is here by approved. 2469-15  AN ORDINANCE AMENDING AND SUPPLEMENTING  CHAPTER 7, TRAFFIC, SUBSECTION 7-20, STOP INTERSECTIONS,  OF   THE REVISED GENERAL ORDINANCES OF  THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  BE IT ORDAINED, by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange, in the  County of Essex, State of New Jersey, that   Sheffield Terrace and Dartmouth Road   shall be  designated as a Stop Intersection pursuant to N.J.S.A. 39:4-8(b) under the Municipal Engineer ’ s  Certification and License Number GE026706 as follows:  Section 1.   The Township Council of the Township of West Orange finds it in the  interest of public safety to designate   Sheffield Terrace and Dartmouth Road   as   a   Stop  Intersection pursuant to N.J.S.A.39:4-8(b).  Section 2.   The Municipal Engineer of the Township of West Orange has submitted  and certified all the legislative requirements pursuant to N.J.S.A. 39:4-8(b) and the criteria as set  forth by the New Jersey Department of Transportation, Bureau of Traffic Engineering have been  met.  Section 3.   Subsection 7-20, Stop Intersections, of the Revised General Ordinances of  the Township of West Orange is hereby supplemented and amended as follows pursuant to the  provisions of N.J.S.A. 39:4-140 ( italic type denotes deletion,   bold type denotes addition) :  Intersection   Stop Signs On:  Sheffield Terrace and Dartmouth Road   Sheffield Terrace  Section 4.   If any provisions of this Ordinance or   application thereof, under any  circumstances, is held invalid, the invalidity shall not affect any of the provisions or  applications of this Ordinance that can be given effect without the valid provision(s) or  application and to this end, the provisions of the Ordinance are severable.

Section 5.   All other Ordinances or parts of Ordinances inconsistent with this  Ordinance are hereby repealed.  Section 6.   Unless another penalty is expressly provided by New Jersey Statute, every  person convicted of a violation of any provision of this Ordinance or any supplement thereto  shall be liable to the penalty of not more that fifty dollars ($50.00) or imprisonment for a term  not to exceed fifteen (15) days or both.  Section 7.   This Ordinance shall take effect upon final passage and publication in  accordance with law and approval by the New Jersey Department of Transportation if necessary.  Robert D. Parisi, Mayor  Jerry Guarino, Council President  ________________________________  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.  Municipal Clerk  Introduced: November 10, 2015  Adopted: November 24, 2015

2469-15  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  The Ordinance, which amends the Traffic Regulations of the Township, designates the  intersection of   Sheffield Terrace and Dartmouth Road   as   a   Stop Intersection with   a Stop Sign   to  be placed on   Sheffield Terrace.   Th is   intersection   is a   “ T ”   intersection   with   Sheffield Terrace  forming the stem   of the   “ T ” . Due to the volume of traffic which travels on   Sheffield Terrace   and  Dartmouth Road , the Township ’ s Engineering Division determined that traffic needed to be  regulated at th is   intersection for the safe movement of traffic through   it . The Engineering  Division recommended designating the intersection as   a   Stop Intersection with   a   Stop Sign on  Sheffield Terrace   to provide for the safe movement of traffic there. Without   a   Stop Sign  Sheffield Terrace   the normal   r ight-of-way rule is not expected to provide reasonably safe  operation of th is   intersection.

2469-15  October 28 , 2015  Mayor and Township Council  Municipal Building  66 Main Street  West Orange, New Jersey 07052  RE:   AMENDMENTS TO CHAPTER 7, TRAFFIC  STOP INTERSECTIONS  Mayor Parisi, Council President Guarino, Ladies and Gentlemen:  The attached Ordinance will designate the intersection of   Sheffield Terrace and Dartmouth Road  as   a   Stop Intersection with   a Stop Sign   on   Sheffield Terrace . Th is intersection   is a   “ T ”   intersection   with  Sheffield Terrace   forming the stem of the   “ T ”   where   a   Stop Sign will be placed. I certify that th is  regulation   is   approved by me after investigation of the circumstances, and that it appears to be in the  interest of safety and the expeditious movement of traffic. Based on this, I request th is   approval of th is  Stop Intersection   pursuant to N.J.S.A. 39:4-8(b).  I performed my investigations of th is   intersection by field inspections, manual traffic counts and  accident investigations, and based on these findings I recommend th is intersection   be designated as   a   Stop  Intersection with   a   Stop Sign on the stem of   the   “ T ”   for th is intersection . Based on the traffic counts, the  Stop Sign will be placed on the minor street as prescribed in the Manual on Uniform Traffic Control  Devices (MUTCD).   A   Stop Sign   on the stem of the   “ T ”   at th is   intersection is   required since the normal  right-of-way rule is not   expected to provide reasonably safe operation of th is intersection .  If you have any questions on this regulation, please call me.  Very truly yours,  Leonard R. Lepore, Municipal Engineer  Director of Public Works  LRL/tp  enc.  cc:   John K. Sayers  Richard D. Trenk  Kenneth Kayser  Police Chief James Abbott  file: ordinance stop intersection sheffield terrace and Dartmouth road

2470-15  AN ORDINANCE AMENDING AND SUPPLEMENTING  CHAPTER 7, TRAFFIC, SUBSECTION 7-32.2,  RESTRICTED PARKING ZONES,  OF THE REVISED GEN E RAL ORDI N ANCES OF  THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  BE IT ORDAINED,   by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange, in the County of  Essex, State of New Jersey as follows:  Section 1.   Subsection 7-32.2, Restricted Parking Zones, of the Revised General Ordinances  of the Township of West Orange is hereby supplemented and amended as follows ( italic type denotes  deletion,   bold type denotes addition):  7-32.2   Restricted Parking Zones. Pursuant to N.J.S.A 39:4-197.6, Restricted Parking Zones, as  defined in N.J.S.A. 39:4-207.6e, are established in front of the following residences as indicated on the  Tax Maps of the Township of West Orange, at the specified locations. Parking within a specific zone is  restricted to the motor vehicle issued a permit pursuant to N.J.S.A. 39:4-197.7. In accordance with  N.J.S.A., 39:4-197.6 parking is permitted in the Restricted Parking Zone except when prohibited in front  of the residence by other sections of this Chapter.  Residence Address   No. of Spaces   Location  28 Cross Street   1   Beginning 122 feet north of the  northerly   curbline of Freeman Street  extended and extending 18 feet  northerly.  Section 2.   If any provision of this Ordinance or application thereof, under any  circumstances, is held invalid, the invalidity shall not effect any other provisions or applications of this  Ordinance that can be given effect without the invalid provision(s) or application(s) and to this end the  provisions of this Ordinance are severable.  Section 3.   All other Ordinances or parts of Ordinances inconsistent with this Ordinance are  hereby repealed.

Section 4.   This Ordinance shall take effect upon final passage and publication in  accordance with law and upon the approval of the New Jersey Department of Transportation if required.  ROBERT D. PARISI, MAYOR  J ERRY GUARINO , COUNCIL PRESIDENT  KAREN J. CARNE VALE, MUNICIPAL CLERK  Introduced: November 10, 2015  Adopted: November 24, 2015

2470-15  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This Ordinance is in response to   a   request from   a handicapped resident   who reside s   at   28 Cross  Street.   By adopting this Ordinance, the Township will establish   a   handicapped parking stall adjacent to  th is   address. Such parking stalls are permissible under the laws of New   J ersey, and they are designated  as a Restricted Parking Zone.

2471-15  AN ORDINANCE AMENDING   CHAPTER 2 , SECTION   52.2 OF THE REVISED  GENERAL ORDINANCES OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE, ENTITLED  “ COMPOSITION OF COMMISSION ”  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   ORDAINED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE ,   in   the   County   of   Essex   and   State   of   New   Jersey,   that  Chapter   2,   Section   52.2   of   the   Revised   General   Ordinances   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange  shall be   amended   and superseded   as follows:  2- 52 PUBLIC   RELATIONS COMMISSION  2- 52.2 Composition   of Commission.  The   Commission   shall   be   composed   of   eight   (8)   members   each   of   whom   shall   be   bona  fide   residents   of   West   Orange.   Each   member   of   the   Township   Council   shall   appoint   one   (1)  member   to   the   Commission   and   the   Mayor   shall   appoint   three   (3)   members   to   the   Commission  each   to   serve   a   one   (1)   year   term   renewable   at   expiration   for   the   remainder   of   the   elected  officials   term.   The   members   shall   be   commissioned   by   the   submission   of   a   letter   to   the  Township   Clerk's   office.   All   vacancies   shall   be   filled   for   the   unexpired   term   only.   Each   member  of   the   Township   Council   and   the   Mayor   shall   reappoint   their   Commissioner   upon   reelection   or  appoint   new   Commissioners.   Each   Public   Relations   Commissioner's   term   expires   with   the  elected   official   who   nominated   them.   The   Township   Council   shall   appoint   one   (1)   Council  member to serve as a liaison to the Commission each year.  Commissioners   may   be   recommended   for   removal   from   the   Commission   for   acts   or  deeds   considered   by   the   Public   Relations   Commission,   Township   Council   or   Mayor   as   contrary  to   the   aims   and   goals   of   the   Public   Relations   Commission;   three   (3)   consecutive   missed  regularly   scheduled   Public   Relations   Commission   meetings   or   attendance   at   less   than   two-  thirds (2/3) of the yearly meetings.  To   remove   a   Public   Relations   Commissioner   from   office   a   written   explanation   of   the  reason   for   said   removal   shall   be   submitted   to   the   Council   President,   Mayor,   nominating   Elected  Official   (if   not   one   of   the   previous)   and   Public   Relations   Commissioner   involved   by   the  Chairperson   of   the   Public   Relations   Commission.   Upon   receipt   of   the   notice   from   the  Commission   the   nominating   elected   official   may   remove   and   replace   said   Commissioner.   A  replacement   shall   be   selected   by   said   nominator   to   finish   the   vacated,   unexpired   term.   (Ord.  1893-03 § 2; Ord. No. 2170-08 § II)  BE IT FURTHER ORDAINED , that the remainder of Chapter 2 shall remain unchanged.  BE   IT   FURTHER   ORDAINED,   that   if   any   section,   paragraph,   subsection,   clause   or  provision   of   this   Ordinance   shall   be   adjudged   by   the   courts   to   be   invalid,   such   adjudication   shall  apply   only   to   the   section,   paragraph,   subsection,   clause   or   provision   so   adjudicated,   and   the  remainder of the Ordinance shall be deemed valid and effective.

BE   IT   FURTHER   ORDAINED ,   that   any   ordinances   or   parts   thereof   in   conflict   with   the  provisions of this Ordinance are repealed to the extent of such conflict.  BE   IT   FURTHER   ORDAINED ,   that   this   Ordinance   shall   take   effect   upon   passage   and  publication in accordance with law.  Karen J. Carnevale   Jerry Guarino  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Robert D. Parisi, Mayor  Introduction: November 24, 2015  Adopted: December 15, 2015

2471-15  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This   Ordinance   is   intended   to   foster   a   close   working   relationship   between   the   Township  Council   and   the   Public   Relations   Commission .   To   ensure   this   working   relationship   continues,  the   amendment   to   Chapter   2,   Section   52.2   includes   the   Township   Council ’ s   appointment   of   one  Council member to serve as a liaison to the Commission each year.  4820-7567-3131, v. 1

pages.text: 263-15  November 11, 2015  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   for   many   years   the   Rock   Spring   Club   ( “ the   Club ” ),   located   at   90   Rock  Spring   Road,   West   Orange,   N ew   J ersey   07052,   has   arranged   for   a   small   helicopter   to   land   on  the   grounds   of   the   Club   to   deliver   Santa   Claus   to   Club   members   and   their   guests   in   attendance  for a holiday celebration;   and  WHERAS,   this   year ’ s   annual   Santa   Claus   event   is   scheduled   for   Sunday,   December   13 ,  201 5 at approximately 1:30 p.m.; and  WHERAS,   Santa ’ s   reindeer,   Rudolph   et   als.,   are   not   available   for   the   trip   at   this   time   of  year as they must be well-rested in preparation for their big Christmas Eve event; and  WHERAS,   Santa   Claus   has   again   graciously   managed   to   make   arrangements   to   travel  by small helicopter to attend the Club ’ s annual holiday celebration; and  WHERAS,   the   New   Jersey   Department   of   Transportatio n,   Division   of   Aeronautics ,  Inspection   &   Aircraft   Operation,   not   wanting   to   be   Scrooge-like,   does   permit   and   has   in   the   past  permitted   helicopter   landings   such   as   these   on   country   club   property   when   approved   pursuant  to its application process; and  WHERAS,   Dennis   Lever,   the   helicopter   pilot   and   agent   for   the   Club,   will   prepare   the  required   Application   for   Temporary   Aeronautical   Facility   License   for   submission   to   the   New  Jersey   Department   of   Transportation,   which   must   be   delivered   no   later   than   December   11 ,  201 5 ,   and   which   must   be   accompanied   by,   among   other   documents   and   things   which   the   Club  will   provide,   a   “ letter,   statement,   or   certificate   from   the   appropriate   governing   body   which   states  there is no objection to the issuance of a temporary license (mandatory for all temps) ” ; and  WHERAS,   for   this   event,   the   appropriate   body   is   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township  of West Orange; and

NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED,   that   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of   West  Orange ,   does   and   hereby   states   that   it   has   no   objection   to   the   issuance   of   a   Temporary  Aeronautical   Facility   License   to   the   Rock   Spring   Club,   located   at   90   Rock   Spring   Road,   West  Orange,   NJ   07052,   for   the   purpose   of   permitting   transportation   and   delivery   of   Santa   Claus   to  the   Club   at   approximately   1 :30   p.m.,   on   Sunday,   December   13 ,   201 5 ,   for   participation   in   the  Club ’ s tr aditional Christmas celebration; and it is further  RESOLVED,   that   this   Resolution   shall   be   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office   for  reasonable inspection in accordance with applicable law.  Karen   J.   Carnevale   Jerry Guarino  Township Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   November 11, 2015  4821-1772-8810, v. 1

264-15  November 10, 2015  Township of West Orange  Resolution   of Support from Local Governing Body   Authorizing t he  Sustainable Jersey   Grant   Application  WHEREAS ,   West Orange,   seeks to optimize   the   quality of life for   countries that have  lost power due to severe, devastating weather conditions   by partnering with the charitable  organization, Liter of Light.  WHEREAS , West Orange   strives to save tax dollars, assure clean land,   air and water,  improve working and living environments ; for   its   residents by ensuring that its  environmental, economic and social objectives are   balanced and mutually supportive; and  WHEREAS, West Orange   is   participating in   the Sustainable Jersey Program; and  WHEREAS,   one of the purposes of the Sustainable Jersey Program is to provide  resources to municipalities to make   progress on   sustainability issues , and the y have  created a grant program called   the   Sustainable Jersey   Small   Grant s   Program ;  THEREFORE ,   through   the   recommendation of the West Orange Environmental  Commission/Green Team, the Town Council   of   West Orange   has   determined that  Township of West Orange   should apply for   the   Sustainable Jersey 2015   Grant.  THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED , that   the Town Council   of   the Township of West  Orange , State of New Jersey, authorize the submission of the aforementioned   Sustainable  Jersey   Grant .  Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk   Jerry Guarino, Council President  Adopted: November 10, 2015

pages.text: 258-15  October 27, 2015  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   there   is   certain   real   property   located   at   Block   35,   Lot   28   in   the   Township,  located on Oakridge   Road   (the   “ Property ” ); and  WHEREAS,   by   adoption   of   Resolution   166-15   on   June   23,   2015,   the   Township   Council  authorized   the   transfer   of   the   Property   from   Robert   C.   Williams   to   the   Township   for   the   sum   of  $1.00 (the   “ Transfer ” ); and  WHEREAS,   at   the   time   of   the   Transfer,   outstanding   taxes   in   the   total   amount   of   $60.33  were due and owing on the Property; and  WHEREAS,   based   upon   the   Township ’ s   acquisition   of   the   Property,   the   Tax   Collector  recommends that the foregoing taxes be cancelled at this time;  NOW,   THERFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of  West   Orange   that   the   Tax   Collector   be   and   is   hereby   authorized,   empowered   and   directed   to  cause   to   be   cancelled   the   amount   of   $ 60.33 ,   representing   the   amount   of   taxes   currently   due  and owing on   the Property ; and it is further  RESOLVED   that   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   shall   be   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office   in  accordance with applicable law.  _______________________   _______________________  Karen J. pages.text: 245-15  October 13 , 2015  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   following   an   advertisement   for   bids   pursuant   to   the   New   Jersey   Local   Public  Contracts   Law,   N.J.S.A.   40A :11-23,   et   seq. ,   for   the   rental   and   operation   of   the   food   and  beverage   concession   stand   at   the   Ginny   Duenkel   Municipal   Pool   (the   “ Concession   Stand  Contract ” ) ,   the   Township   Council   adopted   Resolution   66-15   on   February   24,   2015,   authorizing  the award of the   Concession Stand Contract   to TM Foods ( “ TM ” ) ; and  WHEREAS,   by   adoption   of   Resolution   143-15   on   May   26,   2015,   the   Township   Council  authorized   the   execution   of   the   Concession   Stand   Contract,   a   copy   of   which   is   annexed   hereto  as   Attachment A ; and  WHEREAS,   pursuant   to   the   terms   of   the   Concession   Stand   Contract,   the   Township   may  exercise   the   option   to   extend   the   Concession   Stand   Contract   and   thereby   allow   TM   to   rent   and  operate   the   concession   stand,   for   the   term   beginning   on   May   23,   2016   and   commencing   on  September 16, 2016 , for the sum of $8,400.00 (the   “ Extension Option ” ); and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   wishes   to   exercise   the   Extension   Option ,   and  has confirmed TM ’ s agreement to same;  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of  West   Orange,   that   the   Concession   Stand   Contract   is   hereby   renewed   for   the   term   beginning   on  May 23, 2016 and commencing on September 16, 2016 for the sum of $8,400.00; and  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED   that   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   be   made   available   in   the  Clerk ’ s office in accordance with applicable   law.  Karen J. Carnevale   Jerry Guarino  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   October 13 , 2015  4815-8889-3481, v. 1

pages.text: 246-15  October 13, 2015  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   N.J.S.A.   2B:12-27   authorizes   the   governing   body   of   a   municipality   to  employ   an   attorney-at-law   as   a   prosecutor   to   represent   a   municipality   in   any   matter   within   the  jurisdiction of a municipal court; and  WHEREAS,   in   his   prior   capacity   as   Municipal   Prosecutor   for   the   Township   of   West  Orange   (the   “ Township ” ),   Robert   J.   Candido,   Esq.   handled   a   driving-while-intoxicated   matter  captioned   State v. Hernandez , Summons No. E12-6552/53 (the   “ Hernandez   Action ” ; and  WHEREAS,   a   motion   has   been   filed   in   the   Hernandez   Action   by   which   the   defendant  seeks   the   entry   of   an   order   vacating   her   guilty   conviction   entered   by   the   Court   on   approximately  July 30, 2013 (the   “ Hernandez   Motion ” ) ; and  WHEREAS,   in   light   of   Mr.   Candido ’ s   familiarity   with   this   matter   and   willingness   to  respond   to   and   appear   in   connection   with   the   Hernandez   Motion,   the   Law   Department  recommends   that   the   Township   retain   Mr.   Candido   at   the   fixed   hourly   rate   of   $150   per   hour   to  serve as prosecutor as to the   Hernandez   Action only; and  WHERAS,   upon   retention,   Mr.   Candido   shall   submit   detailed   invoices   to   the   Township  for all services rendered, with a copy to the Township Attorney;  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED ,   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of   West  Orange,   that   Robert   J.   Candido ,   Esq.   shall   be   retained   to   serve   as   prosecutor   in   connection   with  the   Hernandez   Action ; and it is further  RESOLVED ,   that   this   Resolution   shall   be   published   and   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s  Office for reasonable inspection in accordance with applicable law.  Karen J. Carnevale   Jerry Guarino  Municipal   Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   October 13, 2015  4847-4982-6345, v. 1

pages.text: 248-15  October 13, 2015  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   by   agreement   dated   August   1,   2009   (the   “ Lease ” ),   the   Township   of   West  Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   agreed   to   lease   approximately   1,300   rentable   square   feet   of   space   in   a  building   owned   by   Housing   and   Neighborhood   Development   Services,   Inc.   ( “ HANDS ” ),   located  at 555 Valley Street in the Township (the   “ Property ” ); and  WHEREAS,   on   May   12,   2015,   the   Township   and   HANDS   entered   into   a   First  Amendment   to   the   Lease,   which   provided   for,   among   other   things,   a   modification   of   the   rent  payable by the   Township to HANDS for the Township ’ s   use of the Property; and  WHEREAS,   in   order   to   offset   its   new   rent   obligations   and   also   provide   a   headquarters  for   the   West   Orange   Arts   Council   ( “ WOAC ” ),   a   501(c)(3)   organization   created   pursuant   to  Chapter   2,   Section   56   of   the   Township ’ s   Revised   General   Ordinances,   the   Township   desires   to  enter   into   a   sublease   with   WOAC,   in   the   form   annexed   hereto   as   Attachment   A   (the  “ Sublease ” ) ; and  WHEREAS,   pursuant   to   the   terms   of   the   Sublease,   WOAC   has   agreed   to   pay   rent   to   the  Township   in   the   amount   of   $300   per   month   for   the   duration   of   the   Sublease,   which   commences  on September 1, 2015 and expires on August 31, 2019; and  WHEREAS,   in   accordance   with   the   terms   of   the   Lease,   the   Township   has   provided  notification   of   the   Sublease   to   HANDS,   and   has   requested   that   HANDS   provide   its   written  consent to the Sublease;  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   that   the   Mayor   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   execute   the  Sublease   in   the   form   annexed   hereto   as   Attachment   A ,   and   the   Municipal   Clerk   be   and   is  hereby authorized to attest to the Mayor ’ s signature; and it is further

2  RESOLVED   that   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   be   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office   in  accordance with applicable   law.  Karen J. Carnevale   Jerry Guarino  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   October 13, 2015  4853-1310-8265, v. 1

249-15  October 13, 2015  Form SA-22 11/98  NEW JERSEY DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION  DIVISION OF LOCAL AID  AND ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT  RECOMMENDATION OF AWARD  STATE AID PROJECT  BE IT RESOLVED  that   the   Township Council of the Township of West Orange, New Jersey  hereby recommends to the New Jersey Department of Transportation that the contract for  Cherry Street and Belgrade Terrace Roadway Improvements  in the   Township of West Orange , County   of Essex  be awarded to:   Reggio Co nstruction, Inc., 1575 West Street , Fort Lee, New Jersey 07024  whose bid amounted to   $ 585,552.00   subject to the approval of the Department.  That th e Mayor of the Township of West Orange   be and is hereby directed to sign for and on its  behalf the   contract in the prescribed form for said construction.  That the clerk of this body be and is hereby directed to seal said contract with the corporate seal of this body and to attest  to the same. 250-15  October 13, 2015  R E S O L U T I O N  WHEREAS,   the Township Council by Resolution No. 170-15 dated June 23, 2015 awarded a  contract to Reggio Construction, Inc., 1575 West Street, Fort Lee, New Jersey 07024, for Oxford Terrace,  Winding Way and Dartmouth Road Roadway Improvements in the amount of $585,296.25; and  WHEREAS,   to date the contractor has completed all the wo rk associated with the contract  e xcept for the final pavement milling and paving and traffic striping and markings; and  WHEREAS,   the value of the work completed to date which includes drainage facilities, granite  b lock curb, concrete driveway aprons, concrete sidewalks and new handicapped ramps at intersections  is $434,027.00; and  WHEREAS,   two of the streets, W inding Way and Dartmouth Road, are   in the vicinity of the  Gregory Elementary School; and  WHEREAS,   to reduce congestion around the School, the Municipal Engineer recommends these  roads be paved on a Saturday; and  WHEREAS,   work being performed by the paving subcontractor for Reggio Construction, Inc., on  a Saturday has a premium cost associated with it of $8,000; and  WHEREAS,   the Township Engineer by letter dated October 6, 2015 indicated another section of  Winding Way outside the original contract area and located between Sheffield Terrace and   “ upper ”  Sheffield Terrace needs to be milled and resurfaced prior to this winter; and  WHEREAS,   the subcontractors for Reggio Construction, Inc., and Reggio Construction, Inc., will  perform this additional work at the unit prices bid in the contract; and

WHEREAS,   the Township Engineer estimates this additional milling and paving will cost  approximately $20,000; and  WHEREAS,   the Municipal Engineer estimates the cost to complete all the work including the  additional milling and paving and the premium for Saturday paving   at $620,300 which includes  accounting for as built quantities of the work completed to date; and  WHEREAS,   the Municipal Engineer has recommended Change Order No. 1 dated October 6,  2015 with Reggio Construction, Inc. in the amount of $35,000 to authorize additional funding for the  additional milling and paving of Winding Way, the additional cost of paving on Saturday and the  additional cost of the as built quantities to date   be approved ; and  WHEREAS,   sufficient funds exist for this work; and  WHEREAS,   the Township Council concurs in the foregoing.  NOW, THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED,   by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange  that Change Order No. 1 with Reggio Construction, Inc., 1575 West Street, Fort Lee, NJ, 07024 for the  contract Oxford Terrace, Winding Way and Dartmouth Road Roadway Improvements   in the amount of  $35,000 which brings the adjusted contract amount to $620,296.25 or a 5.98 percent increase is  approved.  ___________________________________   ___________________________________  JERRY GUARINO, COUNCIL PRESIDENT   KAREN J. CARNEVALE, MUNICIPAL CLERK  I certify funds are available:  ___________________________________   Account No: 03 2408 15 0060 010  JOHN GROSS, CFO   2015 Street Resurfacing, $35,000.00  Adopted: October 13, 2015

251-15  October 13, 2015  THE TOWNSHIP   OF WEST ORANGE  RESOUTION AMENDING RESOLUTION 217 -15   REQUESTING THE  PLANNING BOARD TO EVALUATE   CERTAIN PROPERTIES IN THE VICINITY OF  EAGLE   ROCK AND   PROSPECT AVENUE   TO DETERMINE IF   THEY   SHOULD BE  DESIGNATED AS AN AREA IN NEED OF REDEVELOPMENT   OR  AN AREA IN NEED OF REHABILITATION  WHEREAS ,   THE   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange   adopted  R esolution   217 -15   on   August   11,   2015   requesting   the   planning   board   to   evaluate  certain   properties   in   the   vicinity   of   eagle   rock   and   prospect   avenue   to   determine   if   they  should   be   designated   as   an   area   in   need   of   redevelopment   or   an   area   in   need   of  rehabilitation ;   and,  WHEREAS ,   upon   further   review   it   has   been   recognized   that   three   contiguous   properties  owned   by   the   Township   of   West   Orange,   Block   107   Lot   1.02   known   as   4   Boland   Drive ,  Block   107   Lot   1.05,   known   as   Crystal   Lake   and   Block   107   Lot   1.06,   known   as   Pro s pect  Avenue   Rear ,   could   be   potentially   impacted   by   the   redevelopment   of   the   contiguous  properties ;   and,  WHEREAS ,   upon   further   review   it   has   been   recognized   that   it   is   advisable   to   include  t hree   additional   lots,   Block   107   Lot   1.02   known   as   4   Boland   Drive,   Block   107   Lot   1.05,  known   as   Crystal   Lake   and   Block   107   Lot   1.06,   in   the   evaluat ion   of   certain   properties   in  the   vicinity   of   eagle   rock   and   prospect   avenue   to   determine   if   they   should   be   designated  as an area in need of redevelopment or an area in need of rehabilitation ;   and,  WHEREAS ,   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40A:12A-4(a),   a   municipal   governing   body   has   the  power   to   cause   a   preliminary   investigation   to   be   made   by   the   Planning   Board   pursuant  to N.J.S.A. 40A-12A-5;   and ,  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   Council   desires   to   have   a   preliminary   investigation   made   on  certain   lands   and   premises   within   the   Township   of   West   Orange   to   see   if   the   area  hereinafter set forth is in need of redevelopment   and/or rehabilitation ;   and ,  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   intend s   to   use   all   those   powers   provided   by   the   Legislature  for use in a redevelopment area,   excluding   the power of eminent domain.  BE   IT   RESOLVED ,   by   the   Council   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange ,   in   the   County   of  Essex,   New   Jersey   does   hereby,   amend   Resolution   217-15   to   include   Block   107   Lot  1.02   known   as   4   Boland   Drive,   Block   107   Lot   1.05,   known   as   Crystal   Lake   and   Block  107   Lot   1.06   in   its   list   of   properties,   that   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40A:12A-4,   request s   and  authoriz es   the   Planning   Board   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange   to   undertak e   a  preliminary   investig ation   as   to   whether   the   properties   identified   in   R esolution   217 -15  and   in   this   Resolution   amending   Resolution   217-15,   be   classified   as   an   area   in   need   of  redevelopment   or an area of rehabilitation ;   and ,  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED   that   the   Planning   Board   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange  shall   conduct   the   aforesaid   investigation   in   accordance   with   the   requirements   of  N.J.S.A. 40A:12A-6;   and,  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED   that   the   redevelopment   area   determination,   if   such   a  determination   is   made,   shall   authorize   the   municipality   to   use   all   those   powers   provided  by   the   Legislature   for   use   in   a   redevelopment   area,   excluding   the   power   of   eminent  domain;   and ,

BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED   that   in   making   its   preliminary   investigation,   the   Planning  Board   shall   make   its   recommendations   based   upon   a   finding   that   the   conditions   as   set  forth in N.J.S.A. 40A:12A-5 are found to be present.  Karen J. Carnevale   Jerry Guarino  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   October 13, 2015

2468-15  AN   ORDINANCE   AMENDING   AND   SUPPLEMENTING   CHAPTER   14  ,   SECTION S  20.  3   AND   20.  5   OF   THE   REVISED   GENERAL   ORDINANCES   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF  WEST   ORANGE,   ENTITLED   “ D UTIES   AND   RESPONSIBILITIES   OF  FORECLOSING CREDITOR ”  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   ORDAINED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF  THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE ,   in   the   County   of   Essex   and   State   of   New   Jersey,   that  Chapter   14  ,   Section s   20.  3   and   20. 5   of   the   Revised   General   Ordinances   of   the   Township   of   West  Orange shall be   amended and   supplemented   as follows:  14-20   DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES OF FORECLOSING CREDITOR  14-20.3   Duty to Report  a.   A   foreclosing   creditor   shall,   within   10   days   of   serving   a   summons   and   complaint,  submit a foreclosure notice to the Township Clerk.  b.   Upon   receipt   of   a   foreclosure   notice,   the   Township   Clerk   shall   provide   a   copy   of   the  foreclosure   notice   to   the   Zoning   Officer   and/or   their   designee(s)   for   enforcement   of  property maintenance regulations.  c.   Once   a   foreclosing   creditor   is   vested   with   legal   title   to   the   property   and   upon   filing  the   deed   with   the   county   clerk ,   the   foreclosing   creditor   shall   simultaneously   provide  the Township Clerk with a copy of the deed.  14-20.5   Responsibility to Maintain Property  In   the   event   that   any   residential   property   becomes   vacant   and   abandoned   subsequent   to   a  foreclosing   creditor ’ s   filing   of   a   summons   and   complaint   but   prior   to   the   vesting   of   title  in   the   foreclosing   creditor   or   any   third   party,   a   foreclosing   creditor   shall   assume   all   duties  imposed   upon   owners   and   operators   of   property,   pursuant   to   Section   8.1   and   Section   8.2  of   this   Chapter   and   Section   5.1   of   Chapter   20,   with   respect   to   the   subject   property.   The  foreclosing   creditor,   within   10   days   of   receiving   notice   that   the   property   is   vacant   and  abandoned,   shall   also   provide   a   copy   of   an   annual   contract   with   the   Towns hip   Clerk   that  sets   forth   the   name   and   contact   information   of   the   representative   who   shall   be   responsible  for   the   care,   maintenance,   security   and   upkeep   of   the   exterior   property   and   ensure  compliance   with   Section   8.1   and   Section   8.2   of   this   Chapter   and   Section   5.1   of   Chapter  20,   with   respect   to   the   subject   property .   The   foreclosing   creditor   shall   also   provide   the  name   and   contact   information   of   the   foreclosing   attorney   and   the   name   and   contact  information   of   the   servicer   of   the   loan.   The   foreclosing   creditor   has   a   continuing  obligation to update all information throughout the foreclosure process.

BE   IT   FURTHER   ORDAINED ,   that   the   remainder   of   Chapter   14   shall   remain  unchanged.  BE   IT   FURTHER   ORDAINED,   that   if   any   section,   paragraph,   subsection,   clause   or  provision   of   this   Ordinance   shall   be   adjudged   by   the   courts   to   be   invalid,   such   adjudication   shall  apply   only   to   the   section,   paragraph,   subsection,   clause   or   provision   so   adjudicated,   and   the  remainder of the Ordinance shall be deemed valid and effective.  BE   IT   FURTHER   ORDAINED ,   that   any   ordinances   or   parts   thereof   in   conflict   with   the  provisions of this Ordinance are repealed to the extent of such conflict.  BE   IT   FURTHER   ORDAINED ,   that   this   Ordinance   shall   take   effect   upon   passage   and  publication in accordance with law.  Karen J. Carnevale   Jerry Guarino  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Honorable Robert Parisi  Mayor  Introduction: October 13, 2015  Adopted: October 27, 2015

2468-15  Legislative History  This   legislation   is   authorized   by   N.J.S.A.   40:48-2.12s  ,   which   permit s   municipalities   to  adopt   ordinances   regulating   the   care,   maintenance,   security   and   upkeep   of   the   exterior   of   vacant  and abandoned residential properties that are the subject of foreclosure actions.  This   amended   legislation   imposes   two   additional   requirements   upon   foreclosing   creditors  in   order   to   regulate   vacant   and   abandoned   residential   properties:   (1)   to   provide   notice   to   the  Township   once   a   foreclosing   creditor   is   vested   with   legal   title   to   the   property;   and   (2)   to   file   an  annual   contract   with   the   Township   Clerk   that   sets   forth   the   name   and   contact   information   of   the  representative   who   shall   be   responsible   for   the   care,   maintenance,   security   and   upkeep   of   the  exterior   property ,   as   well   as   the   foreclosing   attorney   and   servicer   of   the   loan .   Chapter   14,   Section  20.5   also   includes   a   continuing   obligation   clause   for   foreclosing   creditors   to   update   all  information throughout the foreclosure process.  4835-9114-8328, v. 1
