147-15  June   9, 2015  TONNAGE GRANT APPLICATION RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   The Mandatory   Source Separation and Recycling Act, P.L.1987, c.102, has established  a recycling fund from which tonnage grant may be made to municipalities in order to  encourage local source separation and recycling programs; and  WHEREAS,   It   is   the   intent   and   the   spirit   of   the   Mandatory   Source   Separation   and   Recycling   Act   to  use   the   tonnage   grants   to   develop   new   municipal   recycling   programs   and   to   continue  and to expand existing programs; and  WHEREAS,   The New Jersey Department of Environmental Protection has promulgated recycling  regulations to   i mplement the Mandatory Source Separation and Recycling Act; and  WHEREAS,   The   recycling   regulations   impose   on   municipalities   certain   requirements   as   a   condition   for  applying   for   tonnage   grants,   including   but   not   limited   to,   making   and   keeping   accurate,  verifiable records of materials collected and claimed by the municipality; and  WHEREAS,   A   resolution   authorizing   this   municipality   to   apply   for   the   20 1 4   Recycling   Tonnage  Grant   will   memorialize   the   commitment   of   this   municipality   to   recycling   and   to   indicate  the   assent   of   the   Township   Council   to   the   efforts   undertaken   by   the   municipality   and   the  requirements contained in the Recycling Act and recycling regulations; and  WHEREAS,   Such   a   resolution   should   designate   the   individual   authorized   to   ensure   the   application   is  properly completed and timely filed.  NOW   THEREFORE   BE   IT   RESOLVED   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange   hereby  endorses   the   submission   of   the   recycling   tonnage   grant   application   to   the   New   Jersey   Department   of  Environmental   Protection   and   designates   Nick   Salese,   R ecycling   C oordinator   to   ensure   that   the  application is properly filed; and  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED   that   the   monies   received   from   the   recycling   tonnage   grant   be   deposited   in  a dedicated recycling trust fund to be used solely for the purposes of recycling.  Karen J. Carnevale , MUNICIPAL CLERK   Jerry Guarino , COUNCIL PRESIDENT  Adopted:   June   9, 2015

148-15  June 9, 2015  R E S O L U T I O N  WHEREAS,   the Township of West Orange is the owner in fee of property known as Block 158, Lot 39 on  Ridgeway Avenue; and  WHEREAS,   the parcel is approximately 6.2 acres in area; and  WHEREAS,   in 2013 the Township developed the northerly portion of this parcel, consisting of  approximately 4.5 acres, as a park called Ridgeway Park; and  WHEREAS,   the southerly portion of the parcel consisting of approximately 1.7 acres and which  contains two structures, a main house and a carriage house was not improved; and  WHEREAS,   the Township desires to subdivide the lot area containing the two structures from the  Ridgeway Park Section; and  WHEREAS,   a minor subdivision must be prepared by a Licensed Land Surveyor; and  WHEREAS,   the firm of Suburban Consulting Engineers, Inc. of 100 Valley Road, Suite 202, Mount  Arlington, New Jersey, Richard Loede, P.L.S., prepared a boundary and topographic survey for the Township in  2012 for a fee of $6,265.00; and  WHEREAS,   the Municipal Engineer requested a proposal from Suburban Consulting Engineers, Inc. to  prepare two subdivision maps   –   one with a large parcel containing the main house and the carriage house and  the second subdividing the southerly portion into two parcels each with about 150 feet of frontage along  Ridgeway Avenue; and  WHEREAS,   the Municipal Engineer solicited only one proposal since a proposal from any other firm  would require a new boundary survey; and  WHEREAS,   Suburban Consulting Engineers Inc. submitted a proposal dated May 8, 2015 to prepare  the subdivision maps for a fee not to exceed $3,200.00; and  WHEREAS,   the proposal meets with the approval of the Township Engineer; and

WHEREAS,   before this property can be sold authorization by Ordinance of the Township Council is  required; and  WHEREAS,   such authorization will place conditions on the sale including maintaining and refurbishing  the main house and no further subdivision of more than two lots (excluding the park parcel); and  WHEREAS,   the Township Council concurs in the foregoing.  NOW   THEREFORE   BE   IT   RESOLVED,   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange   that   the  Township   be   and   hereby   authorized   to   enter   into   a   Professional   Services   Contract   with   Suburban   Consulting  Engineers,   Inc.,   100   Valley   Road,   Suite   202,   Mount   Arlington,   New   Jersey   to   provide   subdivision   maps   of  Ridgeway   Park   on   Ridgeway   Avenue,   Block   158,   Lot   39   for   a   fee   not   to   exceed   $3,200.00   in   accordance   with  their proposal dated May 8, 2015.  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED   that   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   be   published   in   the   West   Orange   Chronicle  within 10 days of approval in accordance with N.J.S. 40A:11-5(1)(a)(i).  KAREN CARNEVALE, MUNICIPAL CLERK   JERRY GUARINO, COUNCIL PRESIDENT  Adopted: June 9, 2015  I hereby certify funds are available from:  ACCOUNT NO.: 72-2010-00-6900-072 -OPEN SPACE  JOHN GROSS, CHIEF FINANCIAL OFFICER

149-15  June 9, 2015  R E S O L U T I O N  WHEREAS,   the   Township of West Orange   adv ertised   for bids   in the Star Ledger on Saturday ,  May 2, 2015   pursuant to the New J ersey Local Public Contracts Law (N.J.S.   A .   40A:11-1) for the   Removal  and Replacement of the Roofing System, West Orange Police and Justice Building, 60 Main Street ; and  WHEREAS,   at the date ,   time and place advertised for the opening of said bids, the Township did  receive   five   bids; and  WHEREAS,   the Township Engineer reported that the   lowest   bidder   Build Rite LLC of 16  Darlington Drive, Wayne, New Jersey 07470,   has strictly complied with the bid specifications and is the  lowest responsible bidder ; and  WHEREAS,   the Township Council of the Township of West Orange has considered said bids and  has further considered the recommendation of the Township Engineer as to the award of said bid .  NOW, THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED   by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange  that the contract for the   Removal and Replacement of the Roofing System, West Orange Police and  Justice Building, 60 Main Street   be   awarded to:  BUILD RITE LLC  16 DARLINGTON DRIVE  WAYNE , NEW JERSEY 07 470  in the amount   $1 55,580.00 ,   for   Base Bid   1 ,   $127,580 ,   removing and replacing flat roof system ,   Alternate  1 ,   $28,000.00 ,   removing and replacing shingle roof system and a unit price of $24.50 for removing and  replacing deteriorated wood blocking .

BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED   that Certified Checks and/or Bid   B onds of all except the lowest  responsible bidder be forthwith returned, and the Mayor and Township Clerk be and they her e by are  respectively authorized to execute and attest a contract for the foregoing.  KAREN CARNEVALE, MUNICIPAL CLERK   JERRY GUARINO , COUNCIL PRESIDENT  I hereby certify funds are available from:   ACCOUNT NO.   2014 CAPITAL BUDGET  NEW ROOF POLICE BUILDING   - $155,580  03-2407-14-0020-030  JOHN GROSS, CHIEF FINANCIAL OFFICER

pages.text: 150-15  June 9, 2015  RESOLUTION  AUTHORIZING THE AUCTION OF SURPLUS TOWNSHIP EQUIPMENT  WHEREAS,   certain personal property of the Township of West Orange has  become obsolete and is no longer fit or needed for public use; and  WHEREAS , a list vehicles, to be sold as one lot, has been provided by the West  Orange Department of Public Works and has been itemized and described in the  attachment Schedule (1) hereto; and  WHEREAS,   pursuant to the Local Public Contracts Law N.J.S.A. 40A:11-36 (1) when  the fair estimated value of the property to be sold exceeds 15 percent of the bid  threshold in any one sale, it shall be advertised and sold at public sale to the  highest bidder; and  NOW, THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED   by the West Orange Township Council and  hereby in accordance with N.J.S.A. 40A:11-36 (1) etc., as follows:  Such personal property shall be advertised to receive sealed bids and sold to the  highest bidder. The Township of West Orange is not responsible for the quality,  condition or operability of listed personal property for sale. The auction date will  be Wednesday June 24, 2015 at 11:00 A.M. and will be held at the West Orange  Department of Public Works, 25 Lakeside Avenue,  West Orange, New Jersey.  All Moneys received from the sale shall be paid into the General Municipal  Treasury and shall be so accounted and certified by the Chief Financial Officer.  Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk   Jerry Guarino, Council President  Adopted: June 9, 2015

documents.title: REJECTING PROPOSALS FOR EMERGENCY MEDICAL SERVICES AND ASSIGNING SERVICES TO WEST OR

pages.text: 151-15  June 9, 2015  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   t he   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   is   authorized   to   provide  emergency   medical   services   ( “ EMS   Services ” )   for   the   residents   of   the   Township   and   others  within its boundaries under the general police powers granted pursuant to   N.J.S.A.   40:48-2; and  WHEREAS ,   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40A:11-4.1(f)   of   the   Local   Public   Contracts   Law,  contracting   units   are   authorized   to   utilize   competitive   contracting   for   the   procurement   of  emergency medical services;  WHEREAS ,   on   October   1,   2013   the   Township   Council   adopted   Resolution   #180-13  authorizing   the   use   of   competitive   contracting   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40A:11-4.3   for   the  procurement of a contract for emergency medical services   (the   “ EMS Contract ” )   ;  WHEREAS ,   on   January   22,   2014,   the   Township   issued   a   Request   for   Competitive  Contracting Proposals ( “ RFP ” ) for Emergency Medical Services; and  WHEREAS ,   on   April   22,   2014,   the   Township   advertised   and   issued   Addendum   5  amending various provisions of the RFP; and  WHEREAS,   on   Tuesday,   May   13,   2014,   the   Township   received   two   (2)   proposals   from  the following vendors:  Fuchs EMS & Medical Transportation Services ( “ Fuchs ” )  16-00 Highway 208 Suite LL3  Fair Lawn, NJ 07410  Monmouth-Ocean Hospital Service Corporation ( “ M ONOC ” )  4806 Megil Road  Neptune, NJ 07753  and  WHEREAS ,   pursuant   to   the   Local   Public   Contracts   Law   and   the   New   Jersey  Administrative   Code,   the   Township   established   a   five   ( 5 )   person   Evaluation   Committee   (the  “ Evaluation Committee ” ) to evaluate and score the proposals; and

WHEREAS ,   pursuant   to   the   N.J.A.C.   5:34-3.4(f)   each   member   of   the   Evaluation  Committee   executed   a   certification   that   they   do   not   have   a   conflict   of   interest   with   any   of   the  vendors; and  WHEREAS,   MONOC   failed   to   address   the   substantive   changes   to   the   RFP   described   in  Addendum 5, despite receipt and acknowledgement of receipt of Addendum 5; and  WHEREAS ,   under   the   New   Jersey   Supreme   Court   case   of   Meadowbrook   Carting  Company,   Inc.   v.   Borough   of   Island   Heights ,   138   N.J.   307   (1994),   MONOC ’ s   failure   to   respond  to   the   changes   in   the   Township ’ s   RFP   following   issuance   of   Addendum   5:   (1)   denies   the  Township   a   means   of   comparing   the   proposals   under   the   basis   outlined   in   the   RFP  specifications ;   and   (2)   denies   the   Township   assurance   that   MONOC   would   perform   the   EMS  C ontract   consistent   with   the   terms   and   provision   of   the   Township ’ s   RFP,   including   Addendum   5;  and  WHEREAS,   the   Evaluation   Committee   reviewed   and   scored   the   remaining   proposal  from Fuchs; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Evaluation   Committee   issued   an   Evaluation   Report   (the   “ Report ” ),  attached   herein   as   Exhibit   “ A, ”   recommending   that   the   Township   award   of   the   EMS   Contract   to  Fuchs as the remaining responsive proposal; and  WHEREAS,   Fuchs executed a consent to extend its Proposal   into September 2014 ; and  WHEREAS ,   a   proposed   EMS   Contract   was   drafted   consistent   with   the   terms   and  provisions of the Township ’ s RFP and provided to Fuchs for review and execution; and  WHEREAS,   on   September   29,   2014,   Fuchs   advised   that   it   could   not   proceed   with   the  award   of   the   EMS   Contract   absent   material   amendments   which   would   deviate   from   the  advertised   specifications of the Township ’ s   RFP   for EMS Services ; and  WHEREAS ,   such   amendments   are   prohibited   as   a   matter   of   law   under   the   Local   Public  Con tracts   Law,   which   prohibits   material   amendments   to   an   agreement   following   selection   of   a  vendor in a procurement process; and

WHEREAS ,   the   Township   received   no   other   responsive   proposals   in   response   to   its  RFP;   and  WHEREAS ,   the   West   Orange   Fire   Department   has   the   staff   and   resources   to   perform  the necessary EMS services for the Township;  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   has   determined   that   the   EMS   Services   should   be   performed  by the West Orange Fire Department.  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE   that   the   MONOC   Proposal   is   hereby   rejected   in   conformance  w ith the Local Public Contracts Law ; and it is further  RESOLVED,   that   the   Fuchs   has   been   deemed   to   have   withdrawn   its   proposal   submitted  to the Township ; and it is further  RESOLVED,   that   the   Mayor   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   assign   to   the   West   Orange  Fire   Department   the   duties   necessary   to   perform   the   EMS   Services   within   the   Township   of   West  Orange .  Karen   J.   Carnevale   J erry Guarino  Township Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   June 9, 2015  4817-4679-6067, v. 1

TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE  COMPETITIVE CONTRACT REPORT FOR EMERGENCY MEDICAL SERVICES  Overview  The Township of West Orange (the “Township”) is authorized to provide emergency medical services (“EMS Services”) for the residents of the Township and others within its boundaries under the general police powers granted pursuant to N.J.S.A. 40:48-2. Pursuant to N.J.S.A. 40A:11-4.1(f) of the Local Public Contracts Law, contracting units are authorized to utilize competitive contracting for the procurement of emergency medical services. On October 1, 2013 the Township Council adopted Resolution #180-13 authorizing the use of competitive contracting pursuant to N.J.S.A. 40A:11-4.3 for the procurement of a contract for emergency medical services. On January 22, 2014, the Township issued a Request for Competitive Contracting Proposals for Emergency Medical Services (the “RFP”).   The RFP required that the awarded vendor would provide EMS Services for the Township twenty-four (24) hours per day, seven (7) days per week, and 365 days per year for a contract term of one (1) year with options to renew up to five (5) years.   The awarded vendor would supplement the EMS Services provided by and through the   Township’s   Fire   Department,   including   supplying   personnel,   equipment,   vehicles   and supplies to provide basic life support (“BLS”) emergency ambulance service to transport any person who becomes injured or ill within the Township limits and who require EMS Service or transport. The initial RFP was structured whereby each vendor would propose a premium to be paid to the Township based on a range of all dispatched calls received by the vendor. The awarded vendor would be provided a guaranteed minimum call volume.   The awarded vendor would then be authorized to bill patients through Medicare, Medicaid or other insurance for their service, but was barred from billing Township residents who are uninsured or for charges not covered by insurance, a practice referred to a “balance-billing.”   To accommodate the awarded vendor for not engaging in balance-billing, the Township would pay to the awarded vendor a cash subsidy. The awarded vendor was also required to pay rent for the use of the Township’s facility at 25 Mt. Pleasant Place where they would be required to devote one (1) emergency vehicle for use solely within the Township. A mandatory pre-proposal meeting occurred on February 7, 2014 where eight (8) prospective vendors attended to raise and address any questions or issues regarding the contract for EMS Services and the Township’s RFP. On or about March 5, 2014, a lawsuit was filed by a prospective bidder challenging the Township’s RFP.   The prospective bidder alleged that the framework of the Township’s RFP was in violation of the Federal Anti-Kickback Statute, 42 U.S.C. § 1320a-7b(b).   Though the Federal Anti-Kickback Statute had never been applied to a local government entity in this manner and though the question was pending before the Office of the Inspector General within the United State Department of Health and Human Services, the Township revised the RFP with

issuance of Addendum No. 5   1   which amended the structure of the EMS Services contract.   In the revised RFP, the guarantee of the minimum call volume was removed and the payment of a cash subsidy in lieu of balance billing was removed.   Instead, the Township would deduct the costs for services to uninsured residents or for charges not covered by insurance from the annual rent obligation due from the awarded vendor. On April 22, 2014, the Township advertised Addendum No. 5 in the New Jersey Star Ledger. On Tuesday May 13, 2014 the Township received two (2) proposals.   The following vendors submitted proposals: Fuchs EMS & Medical Transportation Services (“Fuchs”) 16-00 Highway 208 Suite LL3 Fair Lawn, NJ 07410 Monmouth-Ocean Hospital Service Corporation (“MONOC”) 4806 Megil Road Neptune, NJ 07753 Pursuant to the Local Public Contracts Law and the New Jersey Administrative Code, the Township created an Evaluation Committee (the “Committee”) to establish weighted criteria for the evaluation of proposals and evaluate and score the proposals.   The Committee was composed of five (5) members including: (1) Anne DeSantis, Purchasing Agent for the Township, (2) John Gross, Chief Financial Officer for the Township, (3) Peter F. Smeraldo, Jr., Fire Chief and OEM Coordinator for the Township, (4) Paul   Wannemacher,   Deputy   Fire   Chief   and   OEM   Deputy   Coordinator   for   the Township; and (5) Robert Martin, Captain of the West Orange Police Department. Pursuant to N.J.A.C. 5:34-4.3(f) each member of the Committee executed a certification that they do not have a conflict of interest as to any of the vendors. In addition, the Committee utilized an advisor to assist in evaluating the proposals: Mark Y. Moon, Esq. from the office of the Township Attorney, Richard D. Trenk, Esq.  1   Addendum #1, Addendum #3, Addendum #4 and Addendum #6 rescheduled the submission date for proposals and did not substantive amend any section of the RFP. Addendum #2 was the issuance of responses to various questions submitted by prospective vendors.

The Process  Prior to the evaluation of the proposals, the Committee met and established weighted criteria for the evaluation of proposals as follows: A.   Technical Criteria – Weighting General Objectives   5 Basic Services   15 Vehicle and Other Equipment and Supplies   15 Quality Assurance   5 Records, Reports and Audits   15 B.   Management Criteria –  Weighting Vehicle Coverage and Staffing   15 Management and Personnel Qualifications   10 References   5 C.   Cost Criteria Weighting Billing   10 Each evaluator was to provide a scoring in each category on a scale of 1 through 5 to determine the base score for each category with 1 representing Poor or Unsatisfactory, 2 representing Weak/Below Average, 3 representing Average or Satisfactory, 4 representing Good/Above Average, and 5 representing Excellent. Each evaluator was then instructed to multiply that base score by the weighting as previously determined by the Committee to determine the Weighted Score.

Evaluation  Upon initial review of the proposals the Committee found that MONOC’s proposal was non- responsive to the substantive changes in the RFP as required in Addendum 5 to the Township’s RFP. As a result, the Committee rejected MONOC’s proposal and did not evaluate or score the proposal at all. Each member of the Committee separately evaluated and scored the remaining proposal submitted by Fuchs. By way of summary, the following sets forth the cumulative score for Fuchs’ proposal: Vendor   Total Base Score   Total Weighted Score Fuchs   121   1260  Conclusion/Recommendation  The Committee recommends that the Contract for EMS Services be awarded to Fuchs E.M.S. & Medical Transportation Services.   In light of the minimal response to the Township’s RFP, the Committee further recommends that the Contract be awarded for a one (1) year term.  4845 ‐ 8647 ‐ 8875,   v.   1

152 -15  June 9, 2015  RESOLUTION  Township of West Orange  Resolution   of Support from Local Governing Body   Authorizing t he  Body-Worn Camera Pilot Implementation Program Grant   Application  WHEREAS ,   law enforcement agencies across the country and worldwide are using body-worn  cameras (BWCs) as a promising tool to improve law enforcement interaction with the public;  and  WHEREAS , preliminary evidence indicates that the presence of BWCs helps strengthen  accountability and transparency, and can assist in de-escalating conflicts, resulting in more  constructive encounters between the police and members of the community; and  WHEREAS , the Bureau of Justice Assistance anticipates making   16   awards t o law enforcement  agencies within the Small Agency Category ( fewer than 250 officers ) for an estimated total of  $2,000,000.00 ; and  WHEREAS,   small-agency   applicants may request no more than $250,000.00   with   the  municipality providing   a 50%   cash match   or in-kind contribution of goods and services that are  directly related to the purpose for which the grant is awarded ;   and  WHEREAS , the West Orange Police Department proposes to submit a Body-Worn Pilot  Implementation Program grant application for a BWC project totaling $666,022.41; and  WHEREAS , the project total includes the purchase of   100 cameras with unlimited storage,  docking stations, built-in technology upgrades, full maintenance on all hardware, and software  access for a five-year period;   and  WHEREAS,   the continuity of long-term costs, such as data storage fees, will be the  responsibility of the municipality;  THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED , that   the Township Council   of   the   Township of West  Orange , State of New Jersey, authorize s   the   Mayor of the Township of West Orange or his  designee to submit   the aforementioned   Body-Worn Camera Pilot Implementation Program Grant  Application .  _____________________________   _____________________________  Karen J. Carnevale, Municipal Clerk   Jerry Guarino, Council President  Adopted: June 9, 2015

Implementation of New Body-Worn Camera Program for Small Agencies  Competition ID: BJA-2015-4171:   This funding category seeks to establish new BWC programs in small police agencies with 250 or less sworn officers. Applicants may request no more than $250,000 under this category. BJA estimates 16 awards will be made in this category for an estimated total amount of $2,000,000.  This competitive solicitation is for law enforcement agencies seeking to establish or enhance BWC Policy and Implementation Programs (PIP). Successful applicants will be responsible for a mandatory   50 percent in-kind or cash match.  Successful applicants will develop and implement policies and practices required for effective program adoption, and will address program factors including the purchase, deployment, maintenance of camera systems and equipment, data storage and access, and privacy considerations.  Goals and Objectives:  - Implementation of a BWC program developed in a planned and phased approach that first achieves broad stakeholder, local political leadership, and community engagement and then leverages partnership input to address policy, training, deployment, and ultimately procurement-related requirements.  -Implementation of appropriate privacy policies that at a minimum addresses BWC program issues involving legal liabilities of release of information, civil rights, domestic violence, juveniles, and victims’ groups.  - Implementation of operational procedures and tracking mechanisms that address the use, review, access, storage, retention, redaction, and expungement of digital voice and audio evidence.  - Training of officers, administrators, and associated agencies requiring access to digital multimedia evidence (DME).  - Adoption of practices and deployment of BWC programs appropriately addressing operational requirements.  Camera-based funding metric formula:  •   Number of Cameras in Project x $1,500 = Max. Requested Funds  •   Max. Requested Funds + Matching Funds = Total Program Cost  •   Note: $1,500.00 is not the expected cost of a body-worn camera, paired with the matching funds it is the expected total program costs for a (1) camera system to include: Camera, Storage, Software, Licenses, Services, Policy Development, Training, etc.  •   The soft-match requirements could be met by line-item data storage costs and partner program expenses such as those incurred by prosecutors, defense bar, or community outreach.

West Orange Police Department Proposal  Maximum request is $250,000.00 with an overall budget of $500,000.00 with the match  Proposal:  •   Unlimited Plan - The most TASER can offer: unlimited storage, technology upgrades built in, and impeccable service.  •   AXON Body camera today, upgraded camera of your choice in 2.5 years, upgraded camera of your choice in 5 years  •   Docking station today, upgraded docking station in 2.5 years, upgraded docking station in 5 years  •   Full maintenance plan on all hardware (cameras + docks) for 5 years  •   Fully customizable access to Evidence.com for all users + software upgrades included at no additional cost for 5 years (upgrades every 4-6 weeks)  •   Unlimited storage for all AXON videos and Evidence Mobile uploads for 5 years  •   Professional Services installation and training (set up assistance, train-the-trainer sessions, and training for all AXON camera and Evidence.com users)  Cost:  $1,660.00 per officer in year 1 $1,240.00 per officer in years 2-5  $3,000.00 additional advanced training Total: $666,022.41  If Awarded:  Project total   $666,022.41  -   Award   $250,000.00  W.O. Cost   $416,022.41

15 3 -15  June 9, 2015  R E S O L U T I O N  WHEREAS,   the Township of West Orange needs to improve the outlet structure at Lake Vincent,  Degnan Park adjacent to Hooper Avenue to comply with current regulations of the New Jersey  Department of Environmental Protection Dam Safety Division; and  WHEREAS,   the Township also needs to incorporate a discharge valve into the outlet structure to  enable the level of the lake to be adjusted and lowered in advance of significant rainstorms to provide  some flood relief; and  WHEREAS,   a section s   of the s horeline   of Lake Vincent near the outlet structure and tot lot in  Degnan Park need to be stabilized; and  WHEREAS,   the services of a Professional Engineering firm are needed to design these  improvements; and  WHEREAS,   the Municipal Engineer solicited proposals from four engineering firms with the  capability and experience to design these improvements; and  WHEREAS,   the firm of Hatch Mott MacDonald (HMM) of   412 Mt. Kemble Avenue, Suite G22,  Morristown, New Jersey 07960   s ubmitted a proposal dated February 24, 2015 with a design fee of  $40,800.00; and  WHEREAS,   the design fee of HMM was lower than the fees of the other respondents: Boswell  Engineering of South Hackensack, N.J.; PS&S of Warren, N.J.; and Neglia Engineering Associates of  Lyndhurst, N.J.; and  WHEREAS,   the Municipal Engineer by letter dated June 3, 2015 recommends a Professional  Services contract be awarded to HMM to provide design services in accordance with their proposal  dated February 24, 2015 for a fee not to exceed $40,800.00; and

WHEREAS,   sufficient funds exist for this work; and  WHEREAS,   the Township Council concurs in the foregoing.  NOW, THERFORE BE IT RESOLVED   by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange  that a Professional Services Contract be awarded to Hatch Mott MacDonald of 412   Mt.   Kemble Avenue,  Suite G22, Morristown, New Jersey to provide professional engineering design services for a new outlet  structure and shoreline stabilization at Lake Vincent, Degnan Park in accordance with their proposal  dated February 24, 2015 for a fee not to exceed $40,800.00.  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED   that   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   be   published   in   the   West  Orange Chronicle within 10 days of approval in accordance with N.J.S. 40A:11-5(1)(a)(i).  KAREN   J.   CARNEVALE, MUNICIPAL CLERK   JERRY GUARINO, COUNCIL PRESIDENT  Adopted: June 9, 2015  I hereby certify funds are available from:   ACCOUNT NO.   72-2010-00-6900-072  OPEN SPACE  JOHN GROSS, CHIEF FINANCIAL OFFICER

pages.text: 154-15  June 9, 2015  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   Gordon   &   Gordon,   P.C.   ( “ Counsel ” )   has   agreed   to   provide   professional  services   for   the   calendar   year   2015,   pursuant   to   the   terms   and   conditions   set   forth   in   the  agreement annexed hereto as Attachment   “ A ”   (the   “ Agreement ” ); and  WHEREAS ,   the   Law   Department   recommends   retention   of   Counsel   for   the   purpose   of  representing the Township regarding tax appeal matters involving environmental issues; and  WHEREAS ,   pursuant   to   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40A:11-5(1)(a)(i),   the   proposed  professional   services   contract   is   subject   to   an   exception   to   the   standard   bidding   procedures   set  forth in the Local Public Contracts Law;  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED,   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of   West  Orange   that   the   Mayor   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   execute   an   agreement,   in   the   form  annexed   hereto,   to   retain   Counsel   to   provide   the   services   set   forth   in   the   Agreement   for   an  hourly rate of $150; and it is further  RESOLVED   that   notice   of   this   award   shall   be   published   and   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s  office in accordance with applicable law.  Karen J. Carnevale,   Jerry Guarino,  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   June 9, 2015  I hereby certify funds are available from:  Account No.  JOHN O. GROSS, CFO  4840-3491-7156, v. 1

documents.title: AUTHORIZING EXECUTION OF AGREEMENT TO RETAIN DR BURCHELL AS COAH EXPERT

pages.text: 156-15  June 9, 2015  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   on   March   10,   2015,   the   Supreme   Court   of   New   Jersey   issued   a   decision   in  the   matter   captioned   In   the   Matter   of   the   Adoption   of   N.J.A.C.   5:96   and   5:97   by   the   New   Jersey  Council on Affordable Housing , 221   N.J.   1 (2015) (the   “ Affordable Housing Decision ” ); and  WHEREAS,   the   Affordable   Housing   Decision   established   a   transitional   process   whereby  municipalities   that   previously   achieved   substantive   certification   from   the   Council   on   Affordable  Housing   ( “ COAH ” )   with   respect   to   their   plans   for   addressing   their   affordable   housing   obligations  ( “ Plans ” )   are   permitted   to   file   declaratory   judgment   actions   seeking   judicial   declarations   that   the  Plans are valid; and  WHEREAS,   COAH   granted   substantive   certification   to   the   Plan   submitted   on   behalf   of  the Township of West Orange (the   “ Township ” ) on December 9, 2009; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   intends   to   file   a   declaratory   judgment   action   (the   “ Action ” )   in  conformance with the transitional process established in the Affordable Housing Decision ;   and  WHEREAS,   in   connection   with   the   Action,   the   Township   will   require   the   services   of   an  expert to opine on the Township ’ s affordable-housing obligations; and  WHEREAS,   Dr.   Robert   Burchell   ( “ Dr.   Burchell ” ) ,   Distinguished   Professor   of   the   Edward  J.   Bloustein   School   of   Planning   and   Public   Policy   at   Rutgers   University,   has   agreed   to   serve   as  an   expert   for   municipalities   seeking   judicial   approval   of   their   Plans   in   conformance   with   the  transitional process established in the Affordable Housing Decision; and  WHEREAS,   Dr.   Burchell   has   agreed   to   provide   his   expert   services   to   multiple  municipalities   through   a   shared   services   agreement   (the   “ Agreement ” ),   the   parties   to   which   will  each be able to retain Dr. Burchell ’ s services at a cost   of   $2,000   per municipality ;

2  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   that   the   Township   is   hereby   authorized   to   file   the   Action,   in  conformance   with   the   transitional   process   established   in   the   Affordable   Housing   Decision,  seeking a judicial declaration as to the validity of its Plan; and be it further  RESOLVED   that   the   Mayor   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   execute   the   Agreement   to  retain   Dr.   Burchell   to   serve   as   the   Township ’ s   expert   in   the   Action   for   the   sum   of   $ 2,0 00;   and   be  it further  RESOLVED   this   resolution   shall   be   published   and   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office   in  accordance with applicable law.  Karen J. Carnevale,   Jerry Guarino,  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   June 9, 2015  I hereby certify funds are available from:  Account No.  JOHN O. GROSS, CFO  4850-6389-8404, v. 1

157-15  June 9, 2015  R E S O L U T I O N  WHEREAS,   the Township of West Orange requires the services of a Professional Engineering  firm and an Architectural firm to design the replacement façade for the Edison Wing of the West Orange  Public Library which collapsed on January 29, 2015; and  WHEREAS,   immediately following the collapse, the Township retained the emergency  Professional Services of Frank H. Lehr Associates of 101 South Harrison Street, East Orange, New Jersey  07018-1799 to provide professional engineering services to evaluate the cause of the collapse and to  make recommendations on the structural condition of the remaining façade; and  WHEREAS,   the services of Frank H. Lehr Associates and their sub-consultant CTS Group  Architects, 17 Commerce Street, Chatham, New Jersey were also obtained to evaluate, analyze and  recommend different façade options including a lightweight thin brick panel system, an EFIS System  (synthetic stucco) and an insulated metal panel system to replace the existing facade; and  WHEREAS,   based on the findings and recommendations of Frank H. Lehr Associates and CTS  Group Architecture, the Township Administration in consultation with the West Orange Library Board of  Directors selected the lightweight thin brick panel system for the new replacement façade; and  WHEREAS,   the Municipal Engineer requested a proposal from the firm of Frank H. Lehr  Associates and their sub-consultant CTS Group Architecture to prepare design plans, design  specifications, bid documents and to provide construction support services for the new lightweight thin  brick panel façade system for the Edison Wing of the West Orange Library; and  WHEREAS,   the firm of Frank H. Lehr Associates with CTS Group Architecture as their sub-  consultant submitted a proposal dated June 3, 2015 to provide the services previously described for a  fee not to exceed $22,100.00; and

WHEREAS,   the Municipal Engineer reviewed the proposal and by letter dated June 3, 2015  recommends a Professional Services Contract be awarded to Frank H. Lehr Associates to provide design  services in accordance with their proposal dated June 3, 2015 for a fee not to exceed $22,100.00; and  WHEREAS,   sufficient funds exist for this work; and  WHEREAS,   the Township Council concurs in the foregoing.  NOW, THERFORE BE IT RESOLVED   by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange  that a Professional Services Contract be awarded to Frank H. Lehr Associates of 101 South Harrison  Street, East Orange, New Jersey 07018-1799 to provide Professional Engineering design services and  Architectural services through their sub-consultant , CTS Group Architecture of Chatham, New Jersey,  for a new lightweight thin brick panel façade for the Edison Wing of the West Orange Public Library in  accordance with their proposal dated June 3, 2015 for a fee not to exceed $22,100.00.  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED   that   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   be   published   in   the   West   Orange  Chronicle within 10 days of approval in accordance with N.J.S. 40A:11-5(1)(a)(i).  Karen J. Carnevale,   Jerry Guarino,  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   June 9, 2015  I hereby certify funds are available from:  Account No.  JOHN O. GROSS, CFO

158-15  June 9, 2015  R E S O L U T I O N  WHEREAS,   the Township of West Orange needs to improve the outlet structure at Lake Vincent,  Degnan Park adjacent to Hooper Avenue to comply with current regulations of the New Jersey  Department of Environmental Protection Dam Safety Division; and  WHEREAS,   the Township also needs to incorporate a discharge valve into the outlet structure to  enable the level of the lake to be adjusted and lowered in advance of significant rainstorms to provide  some flood relief; and  WHEREAS,   a section s   of the s horeline   of Lake Vincent near the outlet structure and tot lot in  Degnan Park need to be stabilized; and  WHEREAS,   the services of a Professional Engineering firm are needed to design these  improvements; and  WHEREAS,   the Municipal Engineer solicited proposals from four engineering firms with the  capability and experience to design these improvements; and  WHEREAS,   the firm of Hatch Mott MacDonald (HMM) of   412 Mt. Kemble Avenue, Suite G22,  Morristown, New Jersey 07960   s ubmitted a proposal dated February 24, 2015 with a design fee of  $40,800.00; and  WHEREAS,   the design fee of HMM was lower than the fees of the other respondents: Boswell  Engineering of South Hackensack, N.J.; PS&S of Warren, N.J.; and Neglia Engineering Associates of  Lyndhurst, N.J.; and  WHEREAS,   the Municipal Engineer by letter dated June 3, 2015 recommends a Professional  Services contract be awarded to HMM to provide design services in accordance with their proposal  dated February 24, 2015 for a fee not to exceed $40,800.00; and

WHEREAS,   sufficient funds exist for this work; and  WHEREAS,   the Township Council concurs in the foregoing.  NOW, THERFORE BE IT RESOLVED   by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange  that a Professional Services Contract be awarded to Hatch Mott MacDonald of 412   Mt.   Kemble Avenue,  Suite G22, Morristown, New Jersey to provide professional engineering design services for a new outlet  structure and shoreline stabilization at Lake Vincent, Degnan Park in accordance with their proposal  dated February 24, 2015 for a fee not to exceed $40,800.00.  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED   that   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   be   published   in   the   West  Orange Chronicle within 10 days of approval in accordance with N.J.S. 40A:11-5(1)(a)(i).  Karen J. Carnevale,   Jerry Guarino,  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   June 9, 2015  I hereby certify funds are available from:   ACCOUNT NO . 72-2010-00-6900-072  OPEN SPACE  JOHN O. GROSS, CFO

159-15  June 9, 2015  R E S O L U T I O N  WHEREAS,   the Township of West Orange entered into a contract with Shauger Property Services, Inc., of  429 Dodd Street, East Orange, N.J. 07017 in the amount or $1,343,714.60 for the Mayfair District Storm Sewer  Improvements; and  WHEREAS,   the work associated with this project impacted Nestro Road, Midro Way, Mayfair Drive,  Rosemont Terrace and Rosemont Drive; and  WHEREAS,   this contract and work followed the first phase of improvement s   known as Mayfair District  Sanitary Sewer Improvements performed by Stacey Construction, Inc., of Clifton, New Jersey which also impacted  Nestro Road, Midro Way and Mayfair Drive; and  WHEREAS,   the completion of the project know n   as Mayfair District Storm Sewer Improvements was  delayed by the installation of new water main s   and water services by the New Jersey American Water Company on  Nestro   R oad and Midro Way during the winter and spring of 2015; and  WHEREAS,   the last major item of construction for the Mayfair District Storm Sewer Improvements is the  pavement restoration on Nestro Road and Midro Way; and  WHEREAS ,   due to the disruption on Nestro Road and Midro Way during the performance of all three  public infrastructure projects, the Township desires to completely repave the entire length   of   each street and their  full width curb to curb; and  WHEREAS ,   the value of all the work performed by Shauger Property Services, Inc. to date is $1,201,098.77  which leaves a balance of $142,615.83; and  WHEREAS,   the Township Engineer negotiated a price of $197,250.00 with Shauger Property Services, Inc.  for pavement restoration of both streets at a unit price of $17.25 per square yard   for   a quantity of 11,000 square  yards plus an allowance for Traffic Directors of $7,500.00; and  WHEREAS,   both the sanitary sewer improvements, the storm sewer improvements and related  engineering fees were financed by funding through the New Jersey Environmental Infrastructure Trust   F und  (NJEITF) in the amount of $2,600,000.00; and

WHEREAS,   the monies of the NJEITF cannot be transferred   to another contract with another contractor  for the pavement restoration; and  WHEREAS,   the Municipal Engineer and the Shauger Property Services, Inc. have agreed   to   a Change Order  in the amount of $197,250.00 to restore the pavement on Nestro Road and Midro Way; and  WHEREAS,   this Change   Or der funds the restoration from the unexpended funds of the contract,  $142,615.83 , and   funds the remainder of $54,634.17 from existing Capital Budget accounts; and  WHEREAS,   the Municipal Engineer by letter dated June 5, 2015 recommends the approval of Change  Order No. 1 as it is beneficial to the neighborhood where the Mayfair District Storm Sewer Improvements were  made; and  WHEREAS,   the Township Council concur s   in the foregoing; and  WHEREAS,   sufficient funds exist for this Change Order No. 1.  NOW THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED,   by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange that   Change  Order No. 1 in the amount of $197,250.00 with Shauger Property Services, Inc., 429 Dodd Street, East Orange, N.J.  07017 for the contract Mayfair District Storm Sewer Improvements is approved for restoring the pavement on  Nestro Road and Midro Way for their entire lengths and widths curb to curb.  BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED   that the unexpended balance to date for this contract   of   $142,615.83 be  authorized to be used for this work and another $54,634.17 be appropriated from existing Capital Budget funds. 2454-15  AN ORDINANCE OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE, COUNTY OF ESSEX, STATE OF  NEW JERSEY, (1) VACATING DESIGNATION OF PUBLIC STREET PURSUANT TO  N.J.S.A.   40:67-18, AND (2) AUTHORIZING INITIATION OF PRIVATE SALE OF REAL  PROPERTY PURSUANT TO   N.J.S.A.   40A:12-13(b) (5)  WHEREAS,   by   Deed   dated   August   6,   1932   (the   “ 1932   Deed ” ),   Ruth   L.   Ross   and   Roland  T.   Ross   transferred   to   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   real   property   commonly  known   and   referred   to   as   Block   80.01,   Lot   38   on   Highwood   Road   within   the   Township   (the  “ Property ” ); and  WHERAS,   the   Property   consists   of   a   non-buildable   lot   that   is   approximately   6,000  square feet in size ; and  WHEREAS,   the   1932   Deed   contained   a   restriction   pursuant   to   which   the   Property   was  to be used as a public street   (the   “ Restriction ” ) ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   has   never   erected   or   maintained   a   public   street   upon   the  Property; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   has   no   intention   of   ever   erecting   or   maintaining   a   public   street  upon the Property; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   has   determined   that   the   public   interest   will   be   best   served   by  releasing the Property from the Restriction; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   h as   determined   that   it   is   in   the   public   interest   to   conduct   a  private   sale   of   the   Property   in   conformity   with   the   procedures   set   forth   in   N.J.S.A.   40A:12-  13(b)(5); and  WHEREAS,   the   Tax   Assessor,   in   consultation   with   the   Township ’ s   Appraiser,   has  determined that the fair market value of the Property is   $3,000.00   (the   “ Fair Market Value ” ) ;  NOW ,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   ORDAINED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   that   the   Property   shall   be   and   is   hereby   released   from   the

2  Restriction,   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40:67-19,   such   that   it   is   forever   discharged   from   any   public  right created by or as the result of the 1932 Deed; and  BE   IT   FURTHER   ORDAINED,   that   the   Township   shall   be   and   is   hereby   authorized,  pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40A:12-13(b)(5),   to   initiate   a   private   sale   to   the   highest   bidder   among   any  bidders   that   own   any   real   property   contiguous   to   the   Property,   subject   to   the   condition   that   the  Property   shall   not   be   sold   for   less   than   the   Fair   Market   Value ,   and   further   subject   to   the  condition   that   the   Property   shall   only   be   utilized   as   a   non-buildable   lot   upon   which   no   principal ,  accessory   or other   structures may ever be erected ; and  BE   IT   FURTHER   ORDAINED,   that   all   owners   of   any   real   property   contiguous   to   the  Property   shall   be   provided   with   notice   of   their   eligibility   to   submit   bids   in   connection   with   this  private   sale   of   the   Property,   subject   to   the   condition   that   the   Property   shall   not   be   sold   for   less  than   the   Fair   Market   Value,   and   further   subject   to   the   condition   that   the   Property   shall   only   be  utilized   as   a   non-buildable   lot   upon   which   no   principal ,   accessory   or   other   structures   may   ever  be   erected,   together   with   a   copy   of   this   Ordinance,   within   twenty   (20)   days   following   passage  and publication in accordance with law ; and  BE   IT   FURTHER   ORDAINED,   that   notice   of   this   private   sale ,   subject   to   the   condition  that   the   Property   shall   not   be   sold   for   less   than   the   Fair   Market   Value,   and   further   subject   to   the  condition   that   the   Property   shall   only   be   utilized   as   a   non-buildable   lot   upon   which   no   principal ,  accessory   or   other   structures   may   ever   be   erected,   shall   be   posted   on   the   bulletin   board   in   the  Municipal Building upon passage and publication in accordance with law; and  BE   IT   FURTHER   ORDAINED,   that   if   any   section,   paragraph,   subsection,   clause   or  provision   of   this   Ordinance   shall   be   adjudged   by   the   courts   to   be   invalid,   such   adjudication   shall  apply   only   to   the   section,   paragraph   subsection,   clause   or   provision   so   adjudicated,   and   the  remainder of the Ordinance shall be deemed valid and effective; and

3  BE   IT   FURTHER   ORDAINED,   that   this   Ordinance   shall   take   effect   upon   passage   and  publication in accordance with law.  Karen J. 2455-15  AN ORDINANCE CREATING A RECLASSIFICATION AND SALARY SCHEDULE OF CERTAIN TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE EMPLOYEES SETTING FORTH THEIR TITLES, CLASSIFICATION AND SALARY RANGES AND ADOPTING A SALARY GUIDE AND ESTABLISHING THE EMPLOYEE'S STATUS AS A TOWNSHIP EMPLOYEE AND UNDER THE GUIDELINES OF THE NEW JERSEY DEPARTMENT OF PERSONNEL. BE IT ORDAINED by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange as follows: SECTION 1. That: an ordinance adopting and creating the reclassification and salary schedules of certain Township of West Orange Municipal employees, setting a salary guide and establishing the employee's status as a Township employee and under the guidelines of the New Jersey Department of Personnel as filed in the office of the Municipal Clerk for the following positions: SEE   APPENDIX 1 - ANNUAL SALARY RANGES - NON UNIFORM TITLES - AFSCME SEE   APPENDIX 2 - ANNUAL SALARY RANGES - NON UNIFORM TITLES - MANAGEMENT - EXEMPT EMPLOYEES SEE   APPENDIX 3 - ANNUAL SALARY RANGES - NON UNIFORM TITLES - TEAMSTER SUPERVISORS SEE   APPENDIX 4 - ANNUAL SALARY RANGES - NON UNIFORM TITLES - TEAMSTER COMMUNICATIONS SEE   APPENDIX 5 - SALARY RANGES - NON UNIFORM TITLES-HOURLY SEE   APPENDIX 6 - SALARY RANGES - ANNUAL STIPENDS SEE   APPENDIX 7 - ANNUAL SALARY RANGES - UNIFORM TITLES - FIRE SUPERIORS SEE   APPENDIX 8 - ANNUAL SALARY RANGES - UNIFORM TITLES - FIREFIGHTERS SEE   APPENDIX 9 - ANNUAL SALARY RANGES - UNIFORM TITLES - POLICE SUPERIORS SEE   APPENDIX 10 - SALARY RANGES - UNIFORM TITLES - POLICE SECTION 2. This ordinance and the titles and salary ranges herein provided shall become effective January 1, 2015 and as indicated above in SECTION 1. SECTION 3. All ordinances and parts of ordinances heretofore enacted which are inconsistent with any provision of this ordinance are, to the extent of such inconsistency, hereby repealed. SECTION 4. This ordinance shall take effect upon final passage and publication in accordance with the laws of the state of New Jersey. ___________________________________________ ROBERT D. documents.title: IMPROVEMENT OF LAKE VINCENT - DEGNAN PARK - HATCH MOTT
MAYFAIR DISTRICT STORM SEWER SANITARY SEWER IMPROVEMENTS
HIGHWOOD ROAD - VACATING PUBLIC STREET AND AUTHORIZING SALE
AWARDING THE CONTRACT FOR THE PRIVATE COLLECTION OF MUNICIPAL COURT DEBT - 2015

pages.text: 142-15  May 26, 2015  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40:23-6.53,   effective   January   16,   2010,   municipalities  are   a uthoriz ed   to   enter   into   contracts   with   private   collection   agencies   or   firms   for   the   collection  of o utstanding municipal court debt; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Administrative   Office   of   the   Court s   (the   “ AOC ” )   issued   the   Supreme  Court   Procedures   Governing   the   Private   Collection   of   Municipal   Court   Debt   (the   “ AOC  Procedures ” ) outlining the requirements for private debt collection companies;  WHEREAS ,   the   AOC   Procedures   authorize   a   private   debt   collection   company   to   pursue  penalties   and   fines   in   certain   enumerated   categories   and   authorized   to   assess   an   additional   fee  of up to 22% of the penalty or fine collected; and  WHEREAS,   on   June   27,   2011,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   obtained  authorization   from   the   Division   of   Local   Government   Services   to   utilize   Competitive   Contracting,  pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40A:11-4.1(k)   to   evaluate,   score   and   award   proposals   for   the   private  collection of municipal court debt; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   Council   adopted   Resolution   #188-11   authorizing   the   use   of  competitive contracting for the Private Collection of Municipal Court Debt; and  WHEREAS ,   the   initial   awarded   Contract   for   private   collection   of   Municipal   Court   Debt  has expired ; and  WHEREAS ,   on   March   20,   2015,   the   Township   advertised   an   RFP   in   the   Star   Ledger   for  a   base   term   of   three   ( 3 )   years   with   an   option   to   extend   the   contract   for   two   (2)   additional   one  year extensions; and  WHEREAS ,   on   April   14,   2015,   in   response   to   the   Township ’ s   RFP,   the   Township  received three (3) proposals:  Pioneer  26 Edward Street  Arcade, NY 14009

MSB Government Services  8325 Tuscany Way  Austin, TX 78754  TaxServ Capital Services NJ, LLC  1313 Dolley Madison Blvd  Suite LL-130  McLean, VA 22101-3926  WHEREAS ,   p ursuant   to   the   Local   Public   Contracts   Law   and   the   New   Jersey  Administrative   Code,   the   Township   created   a   three-person   Evaluation   Committee   (the  “ Committee ” ) to evaluate and score the proposal; and:  WHEREAS ,   pursuant   to   the   N.J.A.C.   5:34-3.4(f)   each   member   of   the   Evaluation  Committee   executed   a   certification   that   they   do   not   have   a   conflict   of   interest   with   any   of   the  vendors;   and  WHEREAS ,   the Committee   reviewed a nd scored the three (3) proposals; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Committee   issued   an   Evaluation   Report   (the   “ Report ” ),   attached   herein  as   Exhibit   “ A, ”   recommending   that   the   Township   award   of   the   Contract   for   Private   Collection   of  Municipal Court Debt to TaxServ as the highest scored proposal; and  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE   that   the   Township   hereby   award s   the   contract   for   the   Private  Collection of Municipal Court Debt   to   TaxServ ; and it is further  RESOLVED,   that   the   Mayor   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   execute   all   documents  necessary   to   effectuate   the   Contract   and   the   Municipal   Clerk   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to  attest to the Mayor ’ s signature; and it is further

RESOLVED,   that   the   original   of   the   C ontract   between   Township   of   West   Orange   and  TaxServ   shall be maintained in the Municipal Clerk ’ s office.  Karen J. Carnevale,   Jerry Guarino  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted: May 26, 2015  4837-6972-5988, v. 1
Exhibit   “A”

TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE  COMPETITIVE CONTRACT REPORT FOR THE PRIVATE COLLECTION OF MUNICIPAL COURT DEBT  Overview  On January 16, 2010, L. 2009, c. 233 was signed into law which authorized municipalities to enter into contracts with private collection agencies or firms for the collection of outstanding municipal court debt. On March 31, 2011, the Honorable Glenn Grant, Director of the Administrative Office of the Courts (the “AOC”) issued the Supreme Court Procedures Governing the Private Collection of Municipal   Court   Debt,   outlining   the   requirements   governing   the   procurement   and   the implementation of contracts under the new law. On June 16, 2011, the Township submitted a request to the Division of Local Government Services to authorize the use of Competitive Contracting, pursuant to N.J.S.A. 40A:11-4.1(k), to evaluate and score proposals submitted by vendors using criteria including price and other factors. On June 27, 2011, the Division of Local Government Services authorized the Township to utilize Competitive Contracting for the evaluation and scoring of proposals. On July 1, 2011, the Township also submitted a draft Request for Proposal (“RFP”) to the AOC for approve to proceed with the procurement process.   On October 4, 2011, the AOC approved the Township’s RFP and authorized the Township to commence the procurement process. On July 26, 2011 the Township Council adopted Resolution #188-11 authorizing the use of competitive contracting for the Private Collection of Municipal Court Debt. On October 14, 2011, the Township advertised the RFP in the Star Ledger for a base term of two (2) years with an option to extend the contract for an additional year. Following receipt of the proposals from five (5) vendors, the Township established a four (4) person evaluation committee (the “Evaluation Committee”) to evaluate and score the proposals.   The Evaluation   Committee   reviewed   and   scored   the   responsive   proposals   and   issued   a   report recommending the award of a contract with TaxServ as the highest scored proposal. The Township Council passed Resolution No. 289-11 authorizing the Township to enter into an Agreement with TaxServ for the Private Collection of Municipal Court Debt. On February 25, 2014, the Township Council adopted Resolution No. 37-14 authoring a one-year extension of the Agreement with TaxServ for the Private Collection of Municipal Court Debt. On March 20, 2015, the Township advertised a second RFP in the Star Ledger for a base term of three (3) years with an option to extend the contract for two (2) additional one year extensions. On April 14, 2015, on response to the second RFP, the Township received three (3) proposals. Pioneer 26 Edward Street

Arcade, NY 14009 MSB Government Services 8325 Tuscany Way Austin, TX 78754 TaxServ Capital Services NJ, LLC 1313 Dolley Madison Blvd Suite LL-130 McLean, VA 22101-3926 Pursuant to the Local Public Contracts Law and the New Jersey Administrative Code, the Township created an Evaluation Committee (the “Committee”) to establish weighted criteria for the evaluation of proposals and evaluate and score the proposals.   The Committee was composed of four members including: (1) Anne DeSantis, Purchasing Agent for the Township (2) John Gross, Chief Financial Officer for the Township (3) Edrie Daniels, Court Administrator for the West Orange Municipal Court and Pursuant to N.J.A.C. 5:34-4.3(f) each member of the Committee executed a certification that they do not have a conflict of interest as to any of the vendors. In addition, the Committee utilized an advisor to assist in evaluating the proposals: Mark Y. Moon, Esq. from the office of the Township Attorney, Richard D. Trenk, Esq.  The Process  Prior to the evaluation of the proposals, the Committee met and established weighted criteria for the evaluation of proposals as follows: A.   Technical Criteria – Weighting Does the vendor utilize software that can accurately track the status of accounts? 7.5 Does the vendor utilize software which will provide the Township and its personnel with access to monitor the status of accounts to be collected by the vendor? 6 What is the level of security utilized by the vendor to insure that the information pertaining to the accounts are protected from unauthorized access? 7.5 Does the vendor’s plan of implementation sufficiently meet the requirements set forth in this RFP? 5

Does   the   vendor’s   plan   of   implementation   comply   with   all federal, state and local laws, rules, and ordinances? 10 Does the vendor provide the best and most effective manner in which to collect all outstanding fines and penalties owed to the Township? 4 B.   Management Criteria –  Weighting Does the vendor’s prior experience reflect the type of services required under this RFP? 15 Does   the   vendor   employ   personnel   with   the   qualifications, experience and knowledge to effectively perform the services required under this RFP? 10 Do the vendor’s references provide positive testimony regarding the vendor’s abilities and qualifications? Do the vendor’s references provide positive testimony regarding the success of the vendor in collecting outstanding debts and obligations? 10 Does   the   vendor   have   a   history   of   violating   federal   law governing debt collection? 10 Does   the   vendor’s   plan   of   implementation   comply   with   the Supreme Court’s Procedures Governing the Private Collection of Municipal Court Debt? 5 C.   Cost Criteria Weighting Does the vendor’s cost proposal comply with the requirements contained   in   the   Supreme   Court   Procedures   Governing   the Private Collection of Municipal Court Debt? 5 Does the vendor’s cost proposal provide the best economic advantage to the Township? 5 Will   the   vendor’s   cost   proposal   result   in   an   excessive Administrative Fee which may affect the likelihood of collecting on the fines and penalties owed to the Township? 5

Evaluation  On April 22, 2015, the members of the Evaluation Committee met, decided on the weighting of the criteria and received a copy of the three (3) proposals from the three (3) vendors.   Upon review of the proposals, no material defects were found to disqualify any of the vendors. Following the initial meeting, a scoring sheet reflecting the weighted scoring was circulated to each member of the Evaluation Committee. Each member of the Committee separately evaluated and scored the three (3) proposals. Attached hereto as Exhibit   “A”   is a copy of the scoring sheets completed by the members of the Committee and a Cumulative Scoring Sheet totaling the scores. By way of summary, the following sets forth the cumulative score for the three (3) remaining vendors: Vendor   Total Score   Total Weighted Score Pioneer   141   1,085 MSB Government Services   144   1,109 TaxServ Capital Services NJ, LLC   165   1,277.5 Members   of   the   Committee   provided   comments   regarding   their   individual   review   of   the proposals.   One member noted that TaxServ’s history of violations was less extensive than the other vendors demonstrating a stronger compliance history.   In addition, the lower percentage rate charged for collections compared to the other vendors could make collection efforts more effective.   Another   member   noted   that   the   record   keeping   software   offered   by   TaxServ appeared more user friendly for the Township’s personnel.  Conclusion/Recommendation  Based on the cumulative scoring from the Committee, the Committee unanimously recommends that the Contract for Private Collection of Municipal Court Debt be awarded to TaxServ Capital Services NJ, LLC.  4814 ‐ 4179 ‐ 7411,   v.   1

documents.title: AUTHORIZING EXECUTION OF AGREEMENT WITH TM FOODS FOR CONCESSION STAND

pages.text: 143-15  May 26, 2015  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   by   adoption   of   Resolution   No.   66-15,   a   copy   of   which   is   annexed   hereto   as  Attachment   A,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   authorized   the   award   of   a  contract,   pursuant   to   a   fair   and   open   process,   to   TM   Foods   ( “ TMF ” )   for   the   seasonal   operation  of   the   food   and   beverage   concession   stand   at   the   Ginny   Duenkel   Municipa l   Pool   (the  “ Concession Stand ” ); and  WHEREAS,   TMF   has   agreed   to   operate   the   Concession   Stand   pursuant   to   the   terms  and   conditions   set   forth   in   the   agreement   annexed   hereto   as   Attachment   B   (the   “ Agreement ” ) ;  and  WHEREAS,   in   accord   with   Township ’ s   bid   specifications   and   TMF ’ s   response   thereto,  TMF   has   agreed   to   pay   to   the   Township   the   sum   of   $8,388.06   for   its   use   of   the   Concession  Stand   during   the   2015   pool   season   and,   in   the   event   of   an   extension   of   the   Agreement   by  Resolution,   to   pay   to   the   Township   the   sum   of   $8,400.00   for   its   use   of   the   Concession   Stand  during   the   2016   pool   season,   and   to   pay   to   the   Township   the   sum   of   $8,475.00   for   its   use   of   the  Concession Stand during the 2017 pool season;  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED,   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of   West  Orange   that   the   Mayor   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   execute   the   Agreement,   in   the   form  annexed hereto; and it is further  RESOLVED,   that   notice   of   this   award   shall   be   published   and   made   available   in   the  Clerk ’ s office for reasonable inspection in accordance with applicable law.  Ka ren J . Carnevale ,   Jerry Guarino,  Township Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   May 26, 2015  4824-0645-0212, v. 1

1   If the Event is rescheduled, and the Township and Owner mutually agree to the Township ’ s use of the property for an  alternative date, all terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement shall apply on the agreed upon alternative date.  145-15  RA   100 Executive Drive   LLC  RA 200 Executive Drive LLC  RA 10 Rooney Circler Owner LLC  c/o RXR Realty, LLC  51 JFK Parkway  Short Hills, New Jersey 07078  May 2 6 , 201 5  In   consideration   of   the   request   by   Township   of   West   Orange   (hereinafter   referred   to   as   the  “ Township ”   or   Licensee ” )   to   RA   100   Executive   Drive   LLC,   RA   200   Executive   Drive   LLC   and   RA  10   Rooney   Circle   Owner   LLC   ( collectively   referred   to   as   the   “ Owner ” )   regarding   use   of   the  Owner ’ s   property   located   at   Executive   Drive,   West   Orange,   New   Jersey   (the   “ Premises ” )   for   the  sole   and   limited   purpose   of   providing   parking   to   or   on   behalf   of   the   Township   in   connection   with  the   Township ’ s   Downtown   West   Orange   Alliance   Annual   Street   Fair   and   related   events   including  the   Slide   the   City   event   ( “ Event ” )   and   the   Owner ’ s   consent   to   same,   the   parties   hereto   agree   as  follows:  1.   Use of the Property .   The Event shall occur during the hours of   6 AM   to   9 PM   on   June  6 th   201 5   and the Owner consents to the Township ’ s use of the Owner ’ s parking lots at  the Premises during the hours of   6 AM   to   9 PM . 1   The Township accepts the Premises  as-is and acknowledges that the Owner does not make any representations and/or  warranties as to the Premises or the fitness of the Premises.   The Township   shall not be  permitted to alter the Premises   in any manner   whatsoever. The Owner shall not charge  the Township any fee associated with the limited grant hereunder.  2.   Permits . The Township represents that the Owner is not obligated to   obtain   any  permits and /or   approvals by any governmental authority   prior to the   Event   and that the  Owner is not participating in the Event in any manner whatsoever .  3.   Signage .   The Township shall be permitted to place or post signs on the Pr emises  beginning on June 5, 2015 for the   sole and limited   purpose   of notifying participants   of  the Event   of parking availability. The   T ownship shall remove any signage by June 8,  2015.  4.   Shuttle Buses.   The Township shall be permitted to arrange for shuttle buses to transport  Event participants f rom   the Pr emises   to other sites related to the Event.   It is agreed that  the shuttle buses are or will be under the exclusive control of the Township.  5.   Indemnification.   The Township covenants and agree to   fully protect, indemnify and  save harmless and defend Owner   and   R XR   Realty ,   LLC ("R XR ")   and their respective  affiliates and subsidiaries (collectively,   “ Indemnified Parties ” ), and any of such party ’ s,  or the Building ’ s, shareholders, directors, officers, agents, invitees, servants, contractors,  members, partners, mortgagees, lessors and employees, from and against any and all  loss, cost (including reasonable attorneys ’   fees and disbursements), damage, injury,  liability claims, liens, demands, taxes, penalties, interest or causes of action of every

nature whatsoever in any manner arising out of or incident to or in connection with the  Event and/or use of the Pr emises   or   the   breach of this Agreement by the   Township , its  agents, employees,   any participants, attendees,   invitees or any third parties . The  Township acknowledges and agrees that the covenant contained herein is a material  inducement to the Owner in granting its consent to the Township hereunder and without  this covenant, the Owner would not consent to the Township ’ s use of the Premises  6.   Assumption of Liability . The Township   hereby acknowledges its assumption of full  and complete responsibility and liability for all losses, claims, suits, actions, damages,  fines, penalties, costs, expenses and liabilities arising from any circumstances even in  circumstances wherein   the Township ’ s   assumption of such responsibility and liability  involves indemnification of indemnified party from the consequences of its own  negligence acts.   The Township   hereby stipulates and aggre e s that no condition  precedent to its indemnification obligation stated herein, whether by way of notice or  otherwise, exists or shall constitute a defense to its obligation to defend, indemnify and  hold harmless the indemnified parties in any circumstance.  7.   Insurance Coverage . The Township   shall maintain liability insurance with coverage  limits reasonably acceptable to Owner and shall name   the Owner and RXR   and its  affiliates and   subsidiaries now or hereafter existing as additional insureds.   In addition,  any bus shuttle service engaged by the Township shall be required to maintain liability  insurance with coverage limits reasonably acceptable to Owner and shall name the  Owner and RXR and its affiliates and subsidiaries now or hereafter existing as  additional insureds.   Prior to the Event,   the Township   will provide a Certificate of  Insurance evidencing such coverage .  8.   Revocation of Consent .   Owner shall have the right to withdraw its consent at any time  for any reason .  9.   Non-Interference . The Township , its agents, employees, any participants, attendees,  invitees or any third parties   shall use best efforts to avoid interfering with the normal  operations of the   office b uilding s located at the Premises (the   “ Office Buildings ” )   and  will use best efforts to minimize interference of the use of the Building and surrounding  area by the tenants and occupants of the Building and agree to complete the Event  within the scheduled time.  10.   Damage . The Township   shall promptly repair any and all damage to the Pr emises  arising out of or in connection with the Township ’ s use of the Premises and return the  Premises to the same condition of the Premises immediately prior to the Township ’ s use  of same..  11.   Clean-Up . The Township   shall clean up all trash and debris from the Event   by no later  than 11:59 am on June 7, 2015.  12.   Use of Name . The Township   and any other entities affiliated with the   Township   may  not use the name of the Owner, or its affiliated companies including R XR   or the  likeness or address of the Building (or any variation thereof) without Owner's express  consent, which may be withheld in Owner's sole discretion .  13.   Traffic Control .   The Township covenants and agrees to cause sufficient traffic  enforcement officials be present on the Premises to monitor and control traffic   to ensure  the safety of Premises .

2449 -15  AN ORDINANCE CREATING CHAPTER 2, SECTION 61 OF THE REVISED  GENERAL ORDINANCES OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE, ENTITLED  “ PEDESTRIAN SAFETY ADVISORY BOARD ”  BE   IT   ORDAINED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF  WEST   ORANGE,   in   the   County   of   Essex   and   State   of   New   Jersey,   that   there   shall   be   created   a  Chapter   2,   Section   61   of   the   Revised   General   Ordinances   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange,  which shall provide as follows:  2-61   PEDESTRIAN SAFETY ADVISORY BOARD  2-61.1 Purpose  The   Township   of   West   Orange   has   established   a   Pedestrian   Safety   Advisory   Board   to  serve   as   a   volunteer   advisory   committee   to   the   Mayor   and   Council   with   regard   to   issues   affecting  pedestrian safety.  2-61.2 Membership  The   Board   shall   be   comprised   of   the   following   eleven   ( 11  )   members,   each   of   whom   shall  be   a   bona   fide   resident   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange,   and   each   of   whom   shall   be   appointed  by the Mayor:  1.   A representative of the Downtown West Orange Alliance;  2.   A representative of the Pleasant Valley Civic Association;  3.   A representative of the Township Council;  4.   A representative of the Thomas Edison National Historical Park;  5.   A superintendent of the Board of Education or D e s ignee ;  6.   The Township Engineer, or his or her representative; and  7.   Police Chief or Designee;  8.   Appointment by the Mayor;  9.   Appointment by the Mayor;  10.   Appointment by the Mayor;  11.   Township Planner;  2-61.3 Term  All members of the Board shall serve until a successor is appointed and each member of  the Board shall serve for a term of three (3) years. Any vacancy shall be filled for the remainder  of the unexpired term only.

2-61.4 Organizational Structure  a.   The Board may adopt bylaws to govern its general operation provided that the bylaws  are approved in advance by resolution of the Township Council.  b.   The   B oard shall mee t at least quarterly .  c.   Minutes of each meeting of the Board shall be taken to serve as a public record.  2-61.4 Staff Assistance  The Board shall have no budget, but shall be assisted and advised from time to time, as  directed by the Mayor, by the Township, the Township Planner, and/or any individual or entity  by the Township to perform grant writing services.  2-61.6 Functions  The functions of the Board shall be as follows:  1.   Investigation and analysis of issues affecting pedestrian safety;  2.   Recommendations to the Mayor and Council regarding initiatives and policies  designated to enhance pedestrian safety;  3.   Participation when authorized by the Mayor, in appropriate regional pedestrian safety  activities; and  4.   Annual meeting to inform the Mayor, Township Council and the general public of the  Board ’ s activities and progress.  BE IT FURTHER ORDAINED,   that the remainder of Chapter 2 shall remain  unchanged.  BE   IT   FURTHER   ORDAINED,   that   if   any   section,   paragraph,   subsection,   clause   or  provision   of   this   Ordinance   shall   be   adjudged   by   the   courts   to   be   invalid,   such   adjudication   shall  apply   only   to   the   section ,   paragraph,   subsection,   clause   or   provision   so   adjudicated,   and   the  remainder of the Ordinance shall be deemed valid and effective.  BE   IT   FURTHER   ORDAINED,   that   any   ordinances   or   parts   thereof   in   conflict   with   the  provisions of this Ordinance are repealed to the extent of such conflict.  BE IT FURTHER ORDAINED,   that this Ordinance shall take effect upon passage and  publication in accordance with law.  __________________________________   __________________________________  Karen J. Carnevale,   Jerry Guarino ,  Municipal Clerk   Council Presi dent  ___________________________________  Robert D. Parisi, Mayor  Introduced:   April 28, 2015  Adopted:   May 12, 2015

2449 -15  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This   legislation   is   designed   to   create   a   eleven   ( 11  )   member   Pedestrian   Safety   Advisory  Board   for   the   purpose   of   advising   the   mayor   and   Township   Council   on   issues   affecting  pedestrian   safety.   Members   of   the   board   will   serve   on   a   voluntary   basis   for   the   terms   of   three   (3)  years.  The   Board,   which   shall   meet   at   least   quarterly,   will   investigate   and   analyze   pedestrian  safety   issues,   present   recommendations   regarding   new   initiatives   and   policies,   and   participate   in  appropriate   regional   pedestrian   safety   activities   when   authorized   to   do   so.   In   discharging   these  functions,   the   Board   will   in   appropriate   instances   be   assisted   by   the   Township   Engineer,  Township   Planner,   Police   Chief   and/or   any   entity   employed   by   the   Township   to   perform   grant  writing services.  4851-4335 1586, v. 1

#2207538 v1  100181-33129  2450-15  BOND   ORDINANCE   AUTHORIZING   VARIOUS   CAPITAL  IMPROVEMENTS   IN   AND   FOR   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF  WEST   ORANGE,   IN   THE   COUNTY   OF   ESSEX,   NEW  JERSEY,   APPROPRIATING   $ 6,298,200   THEREFORE   AN D  AUTHORIZING   THE   ISSUANCE   OF   $ 5,998,280   BONDS   OR  NOTES TO FINANCE   PART OF   THE COST THEREOF.  BE   IT   ORDAINED,   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST  ORANGE,   IN   THE   COUNTY   OF   ESSEX,   NEW   JERSEY   (not   less   than   two-thirds   of   all  members thereof affirmatively concurring) AS FOLLOWS:  SECTION 1:  The   improvements   described   in   Section   3   of   this   bond   ordinance   (the   “ Improvements ” )  are   hereby   authorized   to   be   undertaken   by   the   Township   of   West   Orange,   New   Jersey   (the  “ Township ” )   as   general   improvements.   For   the   said   Improvements   there   is   hereby   appropriated  the   amount   of   $ 6,298,200  ,   such   sum   includes   the   sum   of   $ 299,920   as   the   down   payment   (the  “ Down   Payment ” )   required   by   the   Local   Bond   Law   of   the   State   of   New   Jersey,   constituting  Chapter   2   of   Title   40A   of   the   New   Jersey   Statutes,   as   amended   and   supplemented   (the   “ Local  Bond   Law ” ).   The   Down   Payment   is   now   available   by   virtue   of   provision   in   one   or   more  previously adopted budgets for down payments or capital improvement purposes.  SECTION 2:  In   order   to   finance   the   additional   cost   of   the   Improvements   not   covered   by   application   of  the   Down   Payment,   negotiable   bonds   of   the   Township   are   hereby   authorized   to   be   issued   in   the  principal   amount   of   $ 5,998,280   pursuant   to   the   provisions   of   the   Local   Bond   Law   (the   “ Bonds ” ).  In   anticipation   of   the   issuance   of   the   Bonds   and   to   temporarily   finance   said   improvements   or  purposes,   negotiable   bond   anticipation   notes   of   the   Township   are   hereby   authorized   to   be   issued  in   the   principal   amount   not   exceeding   $ 5,998,280   pursuant   to   the   provisions   of   the   Local   Bond  Law (the   “ Bond Anticipation Notes ”   or   “ Notes ” ).  SECTION 3:  (a)   The   Improvements   authorized   and   the   purposes   for   which   obligations   are   to   be  issued,   the   estimated   cost   of   each   Improvement   and   the   appropriation   therefor,   the   estimated  maximum   amount   of   bonds   or   notes   to   be   issued   for   each   Improvement   and   the   period   of  usefulness of each Improvement are as follows:
